title: Dev branch build fail when libsdl < 2.0.18
body:
```
[17/85] Compiling C object app/scrcpy.p/src_keyboard_inject.c.o                                                                 
FAILED: app/scrcpy.p/src_keyboard_inject.c.o                 
cc -Iapp/scrcpy.p -Iapp -I../app -I../app/src -I/usr/include/x86_64-linux-gnu -I/usr/local/include/SDL2 -I/usr/include/libusb-1.0 -f
lto -fdiagnostics-color=always -pipe -D_FILE_OFFSET_BITS=64 -Wall -Winvalid-pch -Wextra -std=c11 -g -D_REENTRANT -MD -MQ app/scrcpy.
p/src_keyboard_inject.c.o -MF app/scrcpy.p/src_keyboard_inject.c.o.d -o app/scrcpy.p/src_keyboard_inject.c.o -c ../app/src/keyboard_
inject.c                                                                                
In file included from ../app/src/trait/key_processor.h:9:0,
                 from ../app/src/keyboard_inject.h:10,
                 from ../app/src/keyboard_inject.c:1: 
../app/src/input_events.h:57:21: error: ‘KMOD_SCROLL’ undeclared here (not in a function); did you mean ‘KMOD_RALT’?
     SC_MOD_SCROLL = KMOD_SCROLL,                                                                                                   
                     ^~~~~~~~~~~                                                                                                    
                     KMOD_RALT        
```
KMOD_SCROLL is added in 2.0.18 (see https://github.com/libsdl-org/SDL/commit/cb1e20b058f1de55b98f32b7d16cff1f896f88dd)

title: Scrcpy cannot run simultaneously with the Android emulator
body: There will be conflicts when using Scrcpy and NOX or LD Android emulator to run at the same time, and both Scrcpy and Android emulator will crash.
author: tkhs101

title: How do I disable a wireless connection I created for scrcpy?
body:
- [y ] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [ y] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows 10
 - scrcpy version: 1.3
 - installation method: manual
 - device model: Google pixel 4a
 - Android version: android 12

I connected to my phone no problem with a usb and then wanted to try out a wireless connection. I followed a guide to set that up. [(this)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O6WX2bxNA5Y&t=191s&ab_channel=Downloadsource.net). 
A summary of the method: 
- Go to system properties > advanced tab > environmental variables > add a location for scrcpy in path.
- open CMD and type in commands:
```
adb tcpip 5555
adb connect (IP address of phone)
```


But now I want to revert back to just a wired connection since a wireless one is very slow for me but it doesn't let me. When I connect via usb and launch scrcpy.exe it just launches cmd for a second then closes that's it. But when I remove the usb and launch it, it connects wirelessly no problem.

Do I need to delete the port I created? How do I do that? I'm not very literate when it comes to this kind of stuff so I could use some help :). Thanks
When I connect wirelessly, this command shows up: (if that's relevant)
```
scrcpy-server.jar: 1 file pushed. 1.5 MB/s (18570 bytes in 0.011s)
INFO: Initial texture: 1080x2336
```

author: BatausAlinAlexandru

title: Add bash completion script
body:
scrcpy have a lot of options. It would be convenient if they were auto-completed on the command line.

For example:

```bash
scrcpy --loc
# press <tab><tab>
scrcpy --lock-video-orientation
```

https://iridakos.com/programming/2018/03/01/bash-programmable-completion-tutorial

On debian, the existing completion scripts are stored in `/usr/share/bash-completion/completions`.

title: USB port killed 2 of my phones, permanent restart loops/cycles
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows
 - scrcpy version: 1.19
 - installation method: Windows release
 - device model: Google Pixel XL, TracFone LG K31 Rebel 4G LTE Prepaid Smartphone (Locked)
 - Android version: Unsure

**Describe the bug**
After months of using usb-connected scrcpy method, my devices will get stuck in restart loops. The Pixel XL is where I did personal stuff, the LG K31 was my work phone and I did not add/remove any software or change anything except turn on the debugging function to use scrcpy.
With these loops: the phone powers on as usual, showing the Google logo. It stays there for a few moments, or it does the next animation where the logo spins. Then the screen turns black, and it powers on again to show the initial Google logo.
Sometimes it goes through and I'm able to unlock the phone and try to do something for ~10 seconds before the phone goes through the restart cycle again, but this is rare.
Usually I have to leave the phone alone for a day before it stops with this cycle.

I honestly don't believe this is a problem with the software, but rather with the USB port that I was using.
I believe the port is USB 3.2 GEN2, and using this to both charge my phone and have it be a data exchange route could have done my phones in.

I've gone through all the self-help troubleshooting articles provided by both manufacturers to no avail.
**So the main purpose behind posting this is to ask:** does anyone know of any way that I could maybe... connect my phone as USB and access the messaging app while the phone is powered off? Any software tools that can do this, or if it's even possible hardware-wise?
**Nvm, it's not possible and I'll just try other things, probably take it in for a repair/replace and pray they can recover my data. This bug report is just a note then that it's possible for the USB 3.2 GEN2 port to do this.**

On errors, please provide the output of the console (and `adb logcat` if relevant).
N/A

```
Please paste terminal output in a code block.
N/A
```

Please do not post screenshots of your terminal, just post the content as text instead.

title: Scrcpy image freezes after some frames
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows 7
 - scrcpy version: 1.21 (1.20 same issue)
 - installation method: windows release
 - device model: Xiaomi Redmi Note 10 Pro
 - Android version: 12 (ArrowOs [aosp] custom rom)

**Describe the bug**
First i should say that i have to use the windows 7 fix from #2840 
and i also need to use --encoder `OMX.google.h264.encoder` or `c2.qti.avc.encoder`, otherwise i get errors.
Using a different framerate, resolution or switching to the opengl renderer doesnt fix it.

Scrcpy opens up and everything works, till i touch my phone or use scrcpy to swipe or click. As soon as something happens on the display, scrcpy works for a few more frames and then the image (not the software) freezes. I can still input swipes in the software and they get executed on my phone but video is not transmitted anymore. It just stays at whatever frame froze.

`scrcpy --record file.mp4` records untill the point where everything freezes, the ends and no further recording is saved.
`adb shell screenrecord sdcard/test.mp4` records for how long i need it but its all black
The phone/android has an inbuild recording function which works perfectly fine

```
C:\Users\calvin\Desktop\scrcpy_new>scrcpy -m 1024 --encoder OMX.google.h264.encoder
scrcpy 1.21 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
C:\Users\calvin\Desktop\scrcpy_new\scrcpy-server: 1 file pushed, 0 skipped. 25.2 MB/s (40067 bytes in 0.002s)
[server] INFO: Device: Xiaomi M2101K6G (Android 12)
INFO: Renderer: direct3d
INFO: Initial texture: 464x1024
```

title: [Information request] Explanation about specific app DRM
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: MacOS
 - scrcpy version: 1.21
 - installation method: Brew / Using only the server and getting control and video socket data
 - device model: Pixel 2 XL, Samsung S9+
 - Android version: 10

**Describe the bug**

Hello, this is not really a "bug" (or maybe it is?) but an information request about how scrcpy is handling DRM.
Most of the time, when a DRM is happening, we have a black screen or a screen freeze using scrcpy. It is for me the normal state  as the content is protected.

I realized yesterday that on the phones I'm using with scrcpy, I was able to watch Netflix movies and series, even if Netflix is using DRM protection.

I'm starting to question myself about how this is possible, and I wanted to know if you had any information about why this is happening ? Is it a Netflix app problem, or are we trying with scrcpy to bypass DRM sometimes for any reason ?

Thanks for reading :)

title: change mod/set hardware Search key for OTG mode
body:
- [X] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Describe the bug**
Although `mod` key shortcuts aren't supposed to work in OTG mode, it is still possible to use android's hardware keyboard shortcuts for basic usage of-which most are using the `SEARCH` button as a modifier for combinations. 

For me it appears that that key is the super/win key and while combinations done with it give rise to no problems so far using the key alone (as in a single press) for its Search/Assistant application cannot and does not work because it already serves to detach the mouse-keyboard capture in scrcpy.

A possible solution can be to set either the hardware Search key or alternatively map the detach functionality from Super to something else (I have already tried the `--shortcut-mod` option unsuccessfully).

author: Trimanat

title: Automagically connect scrcpy wirelessly on windows
body:
- [X] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [X] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows
 - scrcpy version: 1.12.1+
 - installation method: amy
 - device model: any
 - Android version: any

**Describe the bug**
Not a bug, but a possible solution for using scrcpy wirelessly
Getting scrcpy connect to android phone wirelessly it's not too easy. With the below windows script after attaching your phone with usb for one time everything works until you restart you're phone. 

My primary use case is: Enable tethering on phone, attach pc to this wifi, connect phone via USB to PC, run script. After this is done, running script should connect via wifi without USB connection.

(script it's not really readable, let me know if you have problems using it.)
`echo off
set %ADBPath=
rem set ADBPath=C:\Programs\scrcpy1.21\
rem echo %cd%

setlocal EnableDelayedExpansion
setlocal EnableExtensions

rem to clear local variable and registry use following instructions from command prompt
rem  set AndroidIPLocal=
rem reg add "HKCU\Volatile Environment" /v "AndroidIP"

echo This script will try to connect your phone with this computer via Scrcpy  over wifi! Modify ADBPath to match adb and scrcpy directory (they must be on same dir)
%ADBPath%adb version

rem echo argument %1
echo reading saved phone ip from registry...
for /f "tokens=3" %%i in ('reg query "HKCU\Volatile Environment" /v "AndroidIP" ') do (set AndroidIPLocal=%%i)
rem goto EndOfBatch
rem echo Check if android ip is set
if NOT DEFINED AndroidIPLocal goto CheckPhoneConnected
echo Current saved phone ip %AndroidIPLocal%
echo restarting server
%ADBPath%adb kill-server
echo enabling tcpip mode
%ADBPath%adb tcpip 5555

echo Trying to connect to %AndroidIPLocal% wait...
%ADBPath%adb connect %AndroidIPLocal% | find /i "connected to" 

rem echo %ERRORLEVEL%
goto DoConnectionOk%ERRORLEVEL%
:DoConnectionOk0
	%ADBPath%scrcpy --serial %AndroidIPLocal%
	echo Scrcpy ended correctly
	goto EndOfBatch
:DoConnectionOk1
	echo Connection failed... Verifying correct phone's ip
	%ADBPath%adb usb
	echo attach phone via USB and confirm connection on phone
	pause 
	rem %ADBPath%adb devices
	goto CheckPhoneConnected
:do0
	echo searching ip on wlan0
	FOR /F "tokens=2" %%a in ('%ADBPath%adb -d shell ip addr show wlan0 ^| FIND "inet "') DO (FOR /F  "tokens=1 delims=/" %%b in ('echo %%a') DO (set AndroidIPLocal=%%b:5555) ) 
	goto doWlan0%ERRORLEVEL%
	:doWlan00
		echo searching ip on wlan1
		echo wlan0 not found -> searching on wlan1
		%ADBPath%adb shell ip addr show wlan1 
		echo searching....
		FOR /F "tokens=2" %%a in ('%ADBPath%adb shell ip addr show wlan1 ^| FIND "inet "') DO (FOR /F  "tokens=1 delims=/" %%b in ('echo %%a') DO (set AndroidIPLocal=%%b:5555) ) 
		goto doWlan1%ERRORLEVEL% 
		:doWlan10
			reg add "HKCU\Volatile Environment" /v "AndroidIP" /d "%AndroidIPLocal%" /f
			echo trying to enable tcpip.
			%ADBPath%adb tcpip 5555
			pause Confirm on phone
		:connectViaWifi
			%ADBPath%adb connect %AndroidIPLocal%
			goto connectViaWifi%ERRORLEVEL%
		:connectViaWifi0
			echo connectViaWifi to %AndroidIPLocal%
			%ADBPath%scrcpy --serial %AndroidIPLocal%
			echo Scrcpy ended correctly
			
			goto EndOfBatch
		:connectViaWifi1
			echo Unable to communicate with device. Is computer on same wifi or connected to phone tethering network?
			goto EndOfBatch
		
		:doWlan11
			echo no ip found on wlan1. Ensure that phone and computer are connected to the same wifi (also ok if computer is attached to phone wifi tethering )
			pause	
			goto EndOfBatch
		
	:doWlan01
:do1	
Echo No device found. Connect phone via USB and run script again
goto EndOfBatch
:CheckPhoneConnected
echo check if android is connected via USB
%ADBPath%adb devices
for /f "tokens=2 skip=1" %%i in ('%ADBPath%adb devices ^| find "device"') do (goto PhoneConnected)
goto do1
:PhoneConnected
echo check if it's connected
for /f "tokens=1" %%i in ('%ADBPath%adb devices ^| find "."') do (goto SetIpAndConnectWifi)
echo not connected via wifi
goto do0
:SetIpAndConnectWifi
	for /f "tokens=1" %%i in ('%ADBPath%adb devices ^| find "."') do (set AndroidIPLocal=%%i)
	goto connectViaWifi
	
:EndOfBatch`

body:
- [X] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).

**Environment**

 - scrcpy version: 1.22 (1.18 almost same issue)
 - installation method: Windows Release
 - device model: LENOVO Lenovo TB-X306F
 - Android version: 10

**Describe the bug**

With Scrcpy 1.22

Trying to connect to a Lenovo Tab M10 HD device using a wi-fi connection a first frame of the device is shown on the screen but immediately after Scrcpy stops receiving screen updates from the device. The remote too, control with mouse and keyboard, is completely disabled (nothing happens to the device if I click on the device screen image on Scrcpy). After 1 minute or so Scrcpy close with a Device disconnected warning:

```
C:\Users\Marco\Documents\PassGest-Remote\1.22>scrcpy
scrcpy 1.22 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>

WARN: Device disconnected

With Scrcpy 1.18

The only difference is that with 1.18 most attempts to run Scrcpy end without even the first frame being shown on the screen: Scrcpy stops responding after second INFO line is put on the DOS consolle and after his minute of lag exits with same disconnection warning (and no sign of connection at all with my Lenovo):

C:\Users\Marco\Documents\PassGest-Remote>scrcpy
INFO: scrcpy 1.18 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>

INFO: Renderer: direct3d
INFO: Initial texture: 800x1280
WARN: Device disconnected

Using a different framerate, resolution or switching to the opengl renderer doesnt fix it.
If I try to connect to the Lenovo device with USB connection Scrcpy does his job perfectly (screen updates very fast and I am able to control the device from my PC without problems).
Finally, in the same PC I can display and control, with both versions of Scrcpy, a Meberry M6 Android Tablet, with USB cable and with adb over wi-fi connection too, always without problems.

Any help would be greatly appreciated.

title: IPv6 Usage & Advantages
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
README.md does not describe IPv6 usage or advantages.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add an example of IPv6 usage to README.md.
Permits WAN usage with cellular data only, not blocked/filtered by ISP.
Rooted users (only) do NOT need to physically attach device to computer USB to issue "tcpip" port command;
  it can be enabled on the device:
  sudo /system/bin/setprop  service.adb.tcp.port  5554
(Restore USB:  sudo /system/bin/setprop  service.adb.tcp.port  -1)
  sudo /system/bin/setprop  ctl.restart  adbd
Then on computer (rooted | unrooted), e.g.:
  adb connect [2604:fb91:3c84:d3b2:ac39:64f7:4ebd:2247]:5554
  scrcpy --serial [2604:fb91:3c84:d3b2:ac39:64f7:4ebd:2247]:5554

scrcpy v122 (ZIPfile); Windows 10, Google Pixel 6 Pro, Android 12 (February 2022 update)

author: saidesande

title: Optional root (su, Magisk Zygisk module, etc) for mirroring Android 12+ w/FLAG_SECURE
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

I found https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues/2242 but that seemed to focus on non-root options.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
In Android 12+, it is no longer possible to mirror the output of apps [expecting a `FLAG_SECURE` display](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/view/Display#FLAG_SECURE ).  The non-root fix `scrcpy`  uses now is [to no longer capture secure output on Android 12+](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/commit/f7533e8896c5e362af9a2f31b2b8d849d3207cc2 ).

**Describe the solution you'd like**
For those with root, it'd be nice if `scrcpy` could optionally make use of root access to enable mirroring apps that require secure display output.

Brainstorming:
*  Directly request root access via `su` to do what's needed, if possible
*  Provide an app `.apk` to be installed with system privileges (e.g. as a simple Magisk module)
    * [Magisk now provides Zygisk to inject code into processes](https://github.com/topjohnwu/zygisk-module-sample ), so it may be possible to revert the shell UID check
*  Integration with [`Sui` to provide more natural access to Java APIs as the root user](https://github.com/RikkaApps/Sui )
    * Assuming this may require more effort on `scrcpy`'s side to provide an app (APK?) that requests Sui access

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Disable `FLAG_SECURE` for **all** apps using the LSposed (modern Xposed), or via a Magisk "Zygisk" code injection to the system framework.

This would unfortunately enable *every* app to have this access instead of just allowing the shell, unless a module could be made to revert specifically the shell UID `FLAG_SECURE` check.

**Additional context**
All of this except for maybe using `su` directly if possible sounds rather complicated for a niche of `scrcpy` users.  I'd understand if the immediate reaction to this feature request is to close it and go "Nope, not touching that." :slightly_smiling_face:
author: digitalcircuit

title: Windows, ADB, and OTG
body:
- [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).
 - [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows 10 and 7 (64 bit)
 - scrcpy version: 1.23
 - installation method: Windows
 - device model: multiple
 - Android version: multiple

**Describe the bug**

Apologies if I'm misunderstanding but the existing notes for the OTG function states that ADB is not necessary, but that doesn't actually seem to be the case. Without ADB enabled, "scrcpy --otg" always results in:

"ERROR: Could not find any USB device"

When ADB is enabled, then it works fine - but the interesting thing is that it works even if RSA permission is not granted. Plus, when initializing OTG, scrcpy kills the adb daemon anyways. So maybe there's a flag that's causing it to refuse to start OTG if it doesn't see an ADB device, even though ADB itself isn't needed/used?

Behavior tested and verified to be identical on:

Pixel 5 w/ Android 11
S21 Ultra w/ Android 12
LG G4 w/ Android 6
Moto X4 with Android 9

title: Scrcpy through Bluetooth
body:
Hello,

I would like to set scrcpy over bluetooth from my Nvidia Shield TV to my PC instead of the WiFi - while both the Devices are still connected to the same Wifi - because meanwhile, the WiFi is going to be used by another service that doesn't offer a Bluetooth alternative, so I want to reduce as much as possible the load on the Wifi to make the other service perform as good as possible.

I asked in a previous post but I'm still not clear with how can I do that.

I had this answer (pull 1303):
"Scrcpy does nothing special for bluetooth: if you connect your computer to the device bluetooth network, it should work as usual."

Now, I don't know what's meant by "Bluetooth Network", so I tried to ask around and they told me of "IP over bluetooth" - that I don't know as well - and Bluetooth Tethering, which I know.

Now, I would like to understand, are these 3 things the same thing ("Bluetooth Tethering", "IP over Bluetooth" and "bluetooth network")?

If yes: which of the two devices should be the source? Should Shield connect to PC tethering or should PC connect to Shield tethering? (or it doesn't make the difference and works either way?)

Also, Shield is Android TV based and doesn't have a native Tethering option.
Trying the other way around by opening the Bluetooth Tethering from my PC, it seems that Shield cannot connect to it natively, it has only a normal pair option, which I am also wondering if it can be sufficient for the scrcpy connection.

Do you know if there is some way, maybe if you are aware of an app that may do the job, that allows me to get the needed option working on Android TV without rooting my device? 

And last question: How can I be 100% sure that the connection is passing on Bluetooth and not on Wifi while the two devices are connected to the wifi network for their individual connectivity?

I apologize if my questions may result too basic, silly or out of topic and take this opportunity to thank you in advance for your time and this amazing project, I really love it.

title: Android Studio's `adb` is not the command `adb`.
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

Android Studio's `adb` is not the command `adb`. Need it to be.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

Not to do `sudo apt-get install adb`.

### + in README.md#requirements

For `adb` in Android Studio SDK, do: `sudo ln --symbolic ### /usr/local/bin/adb`.

"###": Should be a path to my/your Android Debug Bridge file.

---

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

**Additional context**
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

title: Forward mouse move without button pressed to device
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

Recently I'm implementing `--forward-all-clicks` option for my client, and I tried to use `ACTION_HOVER_MOVE` action for mouse movement events without button pressed. I was surprised that many Apps will react to it

Related code: https://github.com/yume-chan/ya-webadb/blob/36d44243cc1b40dcaa83c77b0b423add5202c8f7/apps/demo/pages/scrcpy.tsx#L676-L681

```js
handlePointerMove = (e: React.PointerEvent<HTMLDivElement>) => {
    this.injectTouch(
        e.buttons === 0 ? AndroidMotionEventAction.HoverMove : AndroidMotionEventAction.Move,
        e
    );
};
```

![180583c0-2d95-4607-ac1f-14b0349da660](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1330321/155885541-c4410f48-4e43-416a-a910-5315bbcf9e7f.png)

title: Maybe reflection is unneded here
body:
Gradle allows us to add our jars to bootClasspath. At least I do not see if there is read-only or public modificator.
```
project.afterEvaluate {
	project.android.bootClasspath += '/path/to/classpath.jar'
}
```
You can try to build a library containing Android classes (parts of them) and use it in main project.

title: Select Default GPU to render stream in command line argument.
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
display window keeps switching gpus to render stream.
i'm unable to pin scrcpy to a single gpu to use, even setting preferred gpu in windows graphics settings does not work.
i run a multi monitor setup, so normally i move the display window to another monitor.

A clear and concise description of what the problem is. Ex. I'm always frustrated when [...]
if i run another gpu resouce intensive app on the same gpu as scrcpy, both suffers.
scrcpy also changes which gpu to render on when moving to different monitors and maximising window.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.
add arguments to the command line on which gpu to use, and it will render the stream using that gpu.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.
selecting preferred gpu to use in windows graphic settings in windows settings.

**Additional context**

author: Longshoez

title: Mobile app support
body:
There are apps like Samsung SideSync, Realme Link, and similarly, lots of apps with system privilege exists. Why not implement the same for scrcpy? 

Problem: scrcpy is unusable or unreliable without ADB access

Solution: Make a mobile app that acts as a security access point for both mobile and desktops. 

List of possibilities: 
1. Magisk module and root access to allow permissions
2. Flashable zip file to the android phone
3. Setting permissions to the app through ADB commands (similar to how 3rd party navigation gesture apps work)
4. Use screen recorder permissions to share audio and video to the PC. For mouse and keyboard, there are many ways. Accessibility settings can be used to control the phone (not sure how this thing works). One-time ADB command can be used to grant some input permissions to the app. And, I have seen sightings of libusb or similar modules in some Github issues. 

1st 2 ways are meant for advanced developers or custom ROM users, which will be much more reliable and stay as easy as flashing zip from recovery. The other 2 ways are meant for literally everyone who somehow wants to make the scrcpy work wirelessly without voiding the warranty or breaking system updates. 
For the user interface, let the app stay a very basic UI and be open source and the community will shine, making replicas for the scrcpy app with beautiful UI and mods (even Android TVs can be benefitted xD).

author: BasselRaafat

title: systemd units to auto-start scrcpy on connection
body:
Once #3085 is merged, the following files can be used to automatically fire up `scrcpy` for devices as they are connected.

Put the following into `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/systemd/user/scrcpy@.service` for every user who wants `scrcpy` autostarted on connection:
```
[Unit]
Description=scrcpy client for device with serial %i
Documentation=man:scrcpy(1)
ConditionPathExists=%E/scrcpy/autostart/%i

[Service]
Environment="ARGS=--stay-awake --turn-screen-off --power-off-on-close --max-fps 10"
EnvironmentFile=-%E/scrcpy/autostart/DEFAULTS
EnvironmentFile=%E/scrcpy/autostart/%i
ExecStart=/usr/bin/scrcpy -s %i $ARGS
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=2
SuccessExitStatus=2
Environment="DISPLAY=:0"
Environment="XAUTHORITY=%h/.Xauthority"
```
and reload the `systemd` instance: `systemctl --user daemon-reload`

Next, `touch` a file for each device serial number you want to support, e.g.:
```
touch $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/scrcpy/autostart/deadbeef01
```
This means that the `scrcpy@.service` unit will be able to fire up `scrcpy` for the device with the given serial number.

Now, plug in the device, and verify that it works:
```
systemctl --user start scrcpy@deadbeef01.service
systemctl --user stop scrcpy@deadbeef01.service
```

If it does, finally place this into `/etc/udev/rules.d/local-scrcpy-autostart.rules` (as root):
```
ACTION!="remove", SUBSYSTEM=="usb", ATTR{serial}=="?*", TAG+="systemd", \ 
  ENV{SYSTEMD_USER_WANTS}="scrcpy@$attr{serial}.service"
```

and run `sudo udevadm control --reload`. Now unplug and replug the device, and `scrcpy` should start.

You can override the default arguments passed by setting `ARGS=…` in `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/scrcpy/autostart/deadbeef01` for the specific device, or in `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/scrcpy/autostart/DEFAULTS` for all devices that don't have specific configuration.

Released into the Public Domain. Please feel free to put on Wiki, or include in package, as you see fit.

title: On a particular app webform login screen, when scrcpy has been run in -S mode (keep screen off), it will turn off the display of the App off.
body:
**Environment**
 - OS: [Windows 10]
 - scrcpy version: [e.g. 1.16, 1.23]
 - installation method: [e.g. manual build, apt, snap, brew, Windows release...] Windows Release (downloadable zip folder)
 - device model: Xiaomi Mi5s, Sony Xperia Z5 (E6653), 
 - Android version: [e.g. 10]: Both on Lineage 17.1 

**Describe the bug**
When scrcpy (multiple versions) is started with the -S option, when accessing a particular app's login page (7/11's auth.7eleven.com.au), it appears to shut off the mirrored phone's screen (in scrcpy).
In some cases, it appears to want to start the split screen function?
But this appears only if scrcpy is run with -S option (turn physical device's screen off), normal scrcpy appears to be fine
If the device is left untouched, running scrcpy without any options (whether normal or no-console.exe) the display returns with the correct output.
Opening a second scrcpy (-S) window in mode does not resolve the issue, however, if you had left the first instance of scrcpy (-S), then close one of the two instances of scrcpy, the screen comes back on the first instance. When I go through the login screen again, I can't see the webform, but if I use scrcpy (without any options), the webform IS there, just not displayed on the scrcpy (-S) instance.
Can verify this by running a no-option scrcpy side by side to a scrcpy (-S) instance

I also observed the same behaviour with scrcpy 1.23 (which uses .vbs script in place of no-console.exe)
No issues when scrcpy is run without the (-S)

author: cwongtech

title: OTG / HID mode using Bluetooth
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
OTG / Keyboard HID / Mouse HID modes require a physical USB connection to the computer, even though Android supports connecting to Bluetooth HIDs.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Implement an optional Bluetooth HID connection method in addition to the existing libusb method

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Emulating a USB connection - would either require additional hardware (e.g. a USB dongle) or emulation software on the Android device as well.
Sharing devices over USB/IP - no client software available for android devices.
Bluetooth HID emulation (e.g. https://github.com/xenogenesi/hidclient) - requires manual binding & unbinding through the command line and only works on Linux (depends on libbluetooth / BlueZ)

author: cledwynl

title: Suspending scrcpy while keeping device awake
body:
scrcpy has totally revolutionised the way I work with my phone at my desk. Thank you so much for this magnificient tool.

Thanks to #3101, I basically have it always running, but I found that this means it's constantly eating CPU time, hence consuming power. I can use `Mod+p` to power off the display, and that means `scrcpy` also goes to sleep, but then when I want to use the device again, I am forced to enter my password to gain access again to the device.

I wonder if there is a hybrid, a way to marry `--stay-awake` with some sort of suspend mode that the `scrcpy` client-server pair negotiate. The device stays awake (and hence does not lock), but the server stops sending data to the client on request (e.g. `Mod+z`), or after a period of inactivity, until the client wakes the server because of interaction.

title: Isolated build environment
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

In order to compile the project on my machine it's required to install a couple of build dependencies that I don't want to pollute my system.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

I've created a build environment based on docker/podman to do the compilation task isolated in a docker container. This would also allow you to provide a failsafe build environment to all users.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

Do what is described in the readme. My system would end up with dependencies that won't be required anymore, except for that one task from time to time. In the long term I gonna lose track of the dev dependencies.

**My solution**

`Dockerfile-ubuntu-20.04`:

    FROM ubuntu:focal-20220302

    ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

    RUN apt-get update -qq
    RUN apt-get install -qy \
            ffmpeg libsdl2-2.0-0 adb wget \
            gcc git pkg-config meson ninja-build libsdl2-dev \
            libavcodec-dev libavdevice-dev libavformat-dev libavutil-dev \
            libusb-1.0-0 libusb-1.0-0-dev

    WORKDIR /src

    CMD ["/src/install_release.sh"]

`fetch+compile.sh`:

    #!/usr/bin/env sh
    set -o nounset   ## set -u : exit the script if you try to use an uninitialised variable
    set -o errexit   ## set -e : exit the script if any statement returns a non-true return value

    git clone --quiet https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy . || git pull

    docker build -f "Dockerfile-ubuntu-20.04" -t "scrcpy/scrcpy-build-env" .
    docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/src" "scrcpy/scrcpy-build-env" || true

    echo "-----"
    echo "alias scrcpy='./run scrcpy/build-auto'"

## Issues

Currently, your `install_release.sh` combines two processes within a single file: compile and install. Since install requires the sudo command my script would fail (thus the workaround `… || true`). It would be great to have these aspects split into separate files upstream.

---

What do you think?

title: scrcpy constantly forgets my phone
body:
Hello,

The way I connect scrcpy to my phone is like the following:
1. Connect to USB
2. adb tcpip 5555
3. Remove from USB
4. adb connect 192.168.1.99 [A static IP I made in my phone's wifi settings]

And like that, I can easily connect scrcpy to my phone with these lines without connecting to USB using:
1. adb tcpip 5555
2. scrcpy %Options%

It works fine for a few hours, then when I try to connect again afterwards I get this:

```
D:\Programs\scrcpy>adb tcpip 5555
error: no devices/emulators found

D:\Programs\scrcpy>adb connect 192.168.1.99
cannot connect to 192.168.1.99:5555: A connection attempt failed because the connected party did not properly respond after
a period of time, or established connection failed because connected host has failed to respond. (10060)

D:\Programs\scrcpy>adb devices
List of devices attached
*empty*
```

The only way I found to get around this error and get my phone connected again is by reapplying the steps I mentioned at the beginning, i.e. by connecting to USB again. Of course, this solution only fixes the problem for a few hours until scrcpy forgets my phone again.

Is there a way to make scrcpy remember my phone for a longer period of time?

Thanks.

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows 10
 - scrcpy version: 1.23
 - installation method: Windows release
 - device model: Oppo
 - Android version: 10

**Describe the bug**
The [-Sw flag](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy#turn-screen-off) to turn the screen off but keep the device awake it not working as expected over TCP/IP.

title: SCRCPY very slow on WiFi, fast on USB  Started doing this when new WiFi AP was installed
body:
- [X ] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [ X] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: windows 10
 - scrcpy version: 1.23 and 1.18
 - installed from windows zip
 - device model: NREAL Developers Puck
 - Android version: [8.1.0]

**Describe the bug**
I had been using SCRCPY for awhile and it was very fast on USB or WiFi.  Had to replace my wifi gateway with a newer one.  New gateway has all the same settings as the old and all other software works normally on it.

SCRCPY runs same as before when USB connected, but when used over WiFi it is very slow, simple android menu updates taking up to a minute now.  Have checked all the wifi settings and they look normal, no weird routing, no parental controlls, traffic shaping...etc.

USB run looks like this
C:\Users\gcorson\Downloads\scrcpy-win64-v1.23>scrcpy
scrcpy 1.23 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
C:\Users\gcorson\Downloads\scrcpy-win64-v1.23\scrcpy-serve... file pushed, 0 skipped. 86.1 MB/s (41123 bytes in 0.000s)
[server] INFO: Device: QUALCOMM DevKit (Android 8.1.0)
INFO: Renderer: direct3d
INFO: Initial texture: 1440x2560

Wifi setup
adb tcpip 5555
disconnect device USB cable
adb connect 10.0.0.221:5555

C:\Users\gcorson\Downloads\scrcpy-win64-v1.23>scrcpy
scrcpy 1.23 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
C:\Users\gcorson\Downloads\scrcpy-win64-v1.23\scrcpy-serve... file pushed, 0 skipped. 88.3 MB/s (41123 bytes in 0.000s)
adb.exe: error: more than one device/emulator
ERROR: "adb reverse" returned with value 1
WARN: 'adb reverse' failed, fallback to 'adb forward'
[server] INFO: Device: QUALCOMM DevKit (Android 8.1.0)
INFO: Renderer: direct3d
INFO: Initial texture: 1440x2560

Note that adb devices shows only one device at this point

I don't see any errors, scrcpy runs normally but is just hugely slow taking a minute to update the screen

This is NOT a problem on the wifi at my office and was not a problem on my wifi at home before the access point was replaced.

The connection to the device is a 5ghz one and the router and device are within a few feet of each other.

Obviously it's something related to the new WiFi, but what???  Any ideas on how to debug it?

author: vpeinadolopez

title: Multiple video streams for one window
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I have android device with screen resolution 1920x1200, but device encoder support maximum of 1920x1080. So when I run just `scrcpy.exe`, I get
```
INFO: Initial texture: 1920x1200
INFO: New texture: 1920x1080
```
on console, screen is cropped, and touch control does not work.
```
[server] WARN: Ignore touch event, it was generated for a different device size
```

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I am able to run multiple instances of `scrcpy` with half of screen each to bypass encoder limitation:
```
scrcpy.exe --crop=1200:960:0:0
scrcpy.exe --crop=1200:960:0:960
```
But having multiple windows which does not rearrange themselves on device screen rotation is suboptimal solution. It will be nice for `scrcpy` to be able host both halves of screen in the same window.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
To make screen not be cropped, and touch control to work, I can reduce resolution for encoder to 1728x1080:
```
scrcpy.exe -m1728
```
but it would be nice to see the screen with full resolution.

title: Using Samsung Magnifier with scrcpy

The perfect solution for me would be to use Samsung Magnifier from the accessibility settings to enlarge the text, but for some reason scrcpy won't display the magnifier window - while it runs on the phone, scrcpy just shows the screen as if there was no enlargement. Is there any way to work around that and to make scrcpy display that magnifier?
Thank you in advance.

title: [Help] Need help on installing scrcpy through COPR
body:
I'm new to linux. I've been looking for over an hour so it's not for the lack of effort.

How do you install from the COPR repo? I'm using Fedora 35

**EDIT: found the solution myself. I'll close this but leave it here for future noobs.
You must first enable a COPR repo before pulling from it**

run this:
`dnf copr enable zeno/scrcpy`
then you can download with 
`sudo dnf install scrcpy`

title: Error: No available formula with the name "scrcpy" : solución
body:
Error: No available formula with the name "scrcpy"
==> Searching for a previously deleted formula (in the last month)...
Error: No previously deleted formula found.
==> Searching for similarly named formulae...
Error: No similarly named formulae found.
==> Searching taps...
==> Searching taps on GitHub...
Error: No formulae found in taps.**Environment**
 Esto me sucedió en MacBook Pro Mid 2012

Reinstalé Homebrew pero no funcionó e indagando más allá encontré la solución


rm -rf /usr/local/Homebrew/Library/Taps/homebrew/homebrew-core;
Esto va a reiniciar las carpetas de Homebrew (eso entendí) y las dependencias para poder usar "scrcpy"

brew update
Esto actualiza Brew 

Una vez que se actualice Brew hay que instalar Scrcpy


brew install scrcpy

Va a tardar un poco de tiempo pero esto va a terminar de instalar scrcpy

Luego sigue con los demás pasos de instalación de MacOs

**Environment**
 - OS: Arch Linux 5.17.1
 - scrcpy version: 1.23
 - installation method: Arch package manager
 - device model: SM_N960F
 - Android version: 10

**Describe the bug**
Sometimes scrcpy seems unable to negotiate the connection, and breaks usb debugging in the process. Disconnecting and reconnecting the phone presents the devices in `adb devices` again, but it seems to be random whether running `scrcpy` will cause usb debugging to stop working again.

A command to start a click at a position, a command to stop a click at a position.

author: premier69

title: IDR frame interval support
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When the phone screen doesn't change, there will be a big latency for the next IDR frame to be present. 

* I am only using the scrcpy server code(Java), NOT the client code(C).

I have a use case that I want to stream the scrcpy's frames to multiple clients. But the clients might come in different time. So it takes a long time(30-60seconds) for the 2nd client to see the screen when there is no screen movement. The screen will only render when an IDR frame arrives.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I am not sure how to achieve this but it would be great to provide a parameter for developers to specify the IDR frame interval.

I didn't see the option on Android MediaFormat: https://developer.android.com/reference/android/media/MediaFormat. 
I tried modifying the [KEY_I_FRAME_INTERVAL](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/media/MediaFormat#KEY_I_FRAME_INTERVAL) but it didn't help much and I read the [article](https://malleshamdasari.wordpress.com/2013/07/31/difference-between-i-frame-and-idr-frame) that points out I frame is not necessary IDR frame. I also modified [KEY_INTRA_REFRESH_PERIOD](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/media/MediaFormat#KEY_INTRA_REFRESH_PERIOD) and it had no effect.

author: vishnureji

title: Set a specific max_size depending on the encoder by default
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
A clear and concise description of what the problem is. Ex. I'm always frustrated when [...]

The main problem we seems to see with scrcpy is the encoding problem, forcing people to reduce max_size in order to reach a specific value that is working for the device. Otherwise, scrcpy is crashing. Right now, you implemented a fix client side which is retrying with different sizes. This is however not the case server size, meaning that when you don't use the client to get the frames (I'm using directly a socket to read frames), you don't have access to this fix it seems.
I implemented on my side a "fine tuning maxsize" code that is trying to launch multiple times scrcpy to find the biggest value that is working with my device by dichotomy. But this can take some time.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

After looking at a lot of devices, I saw that depending on the encoder, there is in fact a real max size that seems to be relative to which encoder you are using. For example, when using the OMX.google.h264.encoder, I saw that the value found is always the same in my code, and it is 2560 (I think maybe the real max value for this encoder can be a bit higher but less than 2600 as I used a dichotomy but stopped it when it is iterating too much). It means that for this encoder specifically, you can encode a frame with a max size of 2560, no matter what device you are using. ( I tested it with at least 5 different devices models that have a resolution bigger than this value )

With this information, I suppose that it is possible server side to make a matching table of value for each encoder. Except if you know that this value can change (for example an update on the encoder or something like that?), or if you know that an encoder can have a different behaviour between device (which seems to not be the case with OMX.google.h264.encoder), I suppose you can take this value by default if the device is having a bigger resolution. This could avoid the client to check multiple values, and this could avoid doing code on our side if you are using only the server.

I don't know how easy it is to find the max real value each encoder can have, but maybe it is also something that is given by the encoder somewhere ? It would be even better if the encoder was giving back the info.

Please tell me if you think this can be a feature, I would love to discuss about it :)
author: Valkhes

title: How to reduce input delays
body:
The latency of scrcpy is very low.
However, it is not sufficient for playing real-time games.
I understand about the inevitable delay in mirroring the screen.
However, I believe that the input delay can be reduced.
What do you think is the bottleneck for input delay?
In fact, OTG mode has almost no delay.
If we assume that the latency due to communication is significant, what if the raw input, like in OTG mode, is converted on the smartphone side?
Or can you think of a way to reduce the input delay?

title: Screen stalls after computer switches accounts
body:
- [ ] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.
 -  I have two accounts on my computer, when I start scrcpy in account a it works fine, if I switch to account b I find by monitoring that the screen in scrcpy under account a is not refreshed. It seems that the screen is stalled. When I switch back to the a account again, the screen is back on. Is there any way to make it work even after switching accounts. Thank you.

title: Adb connect _gateway:5555 no longer works with Android 12
body:
Hello,

[x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md)
[x] I have checked if there is already a similar issue.

System info:

* Ubuntu 20.04
```
scrcpy --version
scrcpy 1.24 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>

Dependencies (compiled / linked):
 - SDL: 2.0.8 / 2.0.8
 - libavcodec: 57.107.100 / 57.107.100
 - libavformat: 57.83.100 / 57.83.100
 - libavutil: 55.78.100 / 55.78.100
 - libavdevice: 57.10.100 / 57.10.100
 - libusb: - / 1.0.21

```
* Device model: Samsung Galaxy A71
* Installation method: via snap
* Android version: 12

**Note**:  I am using my phone as a hotspot and connecting to it via tcpip

## The issue:
Before upgrading my phone OS to Android 12, I used to connect to it as follows:

```
adb connect _gateway:5555
```

However, yesterday, I have upgraded my phone OS to Android 12 and I can no longer use that command to connect. I get the following error:

```
unable to connect to _gateway:5555: Name or service not known
```

The only workaround I found so far is to go to the Phone settings > About > Status Information  and checking for the IP address.

The IP address is something like: `10.175.221.108` but it keeps changing from time to time. 

Why is `_gateway` is not working as is in previous Android versions? (reminder: I am using the phone as a hotspot, so _gateway should be resolved to the IP address of the phone)


Thanks

Any step wrong with me?

title: Having socket trouble connecting to scrcpy-server
body:
I created this issue from another account but it was marked as spammy so I created this one. My apology for the inconvenience.

I'm trying to create a custom client in C# that can connect to scrcpy-server. However, I'm not familiar with socket programming. My understanding from the server's source code is that the client needs to connect to a Unix-domain socket named "scrcpy". But when I try to connect to scrcpy-server, the client said "Can't assign requested address scrcpy". I have pushed the server file to my Android device and run "CLASSPATH=..." from adb shell.

Can somebody give me the steps necessary to connect to scrcpy-server? Thank you!

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
A clear and concise description of what the problem is. Ex. I'm always frustrated when [...]

It is recommended to package this sndcpy package into scrcpy's release and add instructions in readme, so that it will be more convenient for users to decide whether to enable this feature or not. Moreover, when the Android version is upgraded, sndcpy is likely to be incompatible. The more users, the sooner you can find the problem and give it back to you.

title: scrcpy to remote server doesn't error, but doesn't work either
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: host: `archlinux`, remote: `amazon linux 2` (docker)
 - scrcpy version: `1.23`
 - installation method: `pacman`
 - device model: Samsung galaxy S21 ultra (I tried galaxy S5 as well)
 - Android version: `12`

**Describe the bug**
I'm not even sure if I'm holding it right, I couldn't figure it out from the docs.

I have an `adb` on a remote server I can connect to with `adb -H <ip> -P <port>`, and I connect to it with `scrcpy`, but I get no screen, no errors, and I don't know how to debug it.

The server was started with:
```
$> adb -a -P 5038 nodaemon server start
```

And I try connecting to it with:
```
 $> ADB_SERVER_SOCKET=tcp:172.24.20.11:32323 scrcpy --tunnel-host=172.24.20.11 --tunnel-port=32323 -b2M -m800 --max-fps 15
scrcpy 1.23 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
INFO: Tunnel host/port is set, --force-adb-forward automatically enabled.
/usr/share/scrcpy/scrcpy-server: 1 file pushed, 0 skipped. 191.3 MB/s (41123 bytes in 0.000s)
[server] INFO: Device: samsung SM-G998U1 (Android 12)
```

I don't have `scrcpy` installed on the remote server, only on the local host, and the port `32323` is forwarded and mapped to `5038` on which `adb` is running (and as I said, `adb -H <ip> -P <port>` works)

What more can I provide?

author: TheOneWithTheBraid

title: Sound problem
body:
I don't know why and how, but when I connected my phone to my PC via TCPIP and with the screen off, it put the volume to the maximum. Pretty embarrassing. I always verify the sound is off by playing some music before class, and it was.
It was a good idea to hide the screen and display it only on the PC, but maybe do the same for the sound.
Anyway, the class have had a few seconds of free music, and it'll be a nice anecdote.
author: DamienBuchet

title: Disabling stay awake after not returning to default state
body:
Hey everyone,

Thanks for this great tool and all the effort put into this.

I have used this for a while but now my phone is kind of stuck in the stay awake mode which means that the screen stays on when I am charging the device for example.
What is the ADB command to revert this setting to default Android behaviour?

Thanks for your help

Best regards,

Erhan

author: kalafusiu

title: Scrcpy works when using the terminal, but not in a shell script...
body:
Hi everyone,
so, this is less of a bug report, but more of a noob question, I guess? Let's see:

Using scrcpy via WLAN works quite fine, when executing the following commands via terminal: 
```
adb tcpip 5555
adb connect [MY-IP-HERE]:5555
scrcpy -e -b 20M -S
```

Et voila, the screen is mirrored as supposed.
BUT if I put this very command string in an executable shell script:

```
#!/bin/bash
adb tcpip 5555
adb connect [MY-IP-HERE]:5555
scrcpy -e -b 20M -S
```

The scrcpy command replies “ERROR: Server connection failed”

Anyone knows, why this happens? I am a bit confused. The script invokes literally the exact commands, I enter manually…
With regards
Julius
author: kneckergit

title: support alt-key and more keys
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

I try use termux in scrcpy, and found many keys cannot be sent to termux. Such as alt-key (use other key as MOD key), `<F1>`, `<C-[>`, `<C-]>`, `<C-_>`, `<C-\\>`

(however, `<Esc>` is equal to `<C-[>` and it can be sent. But I prefer to press `<C-[>` in vim not `<Esc>`)

title: Install this software in msys2
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
A clear and concise description of what the problem is. Ex. I'm always frustrated when [...]

I notice <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/BUILD.md#in-msys2> can build this program in msys2. Moreover, how about allowing user to install this program by `pacman -S mingw-w64-scrcpy` in msys2? It will be more convenient for user to install or test.

body:
- [ ] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
A clear and concise description of what the problem is. Ex. I'm always frustrated when [...]

A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

**Additional context**
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

left_down(handle: HWND, x: int, y: int):
    ""

    Args:
        handle (HWND): 
        x (int): 
        y (int): 
    """
    # https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/inputdev/wm-lbuttondown
    wparam = 0
    lparam = y << 16 | x
    PostMessageW(handle, WM_LBUTTONDOWN, wparam, lparam)


 left_up(handle: HWND, x: int, y: int):
    ""

    Args:
        handle (HWND): 
        x (int): 
        y (int): 
    """
    # https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/inputdev/wm-lbuttonup
    wparam = 0
    lparam = y << 16 | x
    PostMessageW(handle, WM_LBUTTONUP, wparam, lparam)

I used 
do 
left_down hwnd,100,100
left_up hwnd，100，100
sleep 100
loop

I click the coordinate points in the screen through the API loop. If the mouse position is in the image of scrcpy, the clicked coordinates are not offset. If you move the mouse to other programs, you can observe that the mouse click points displayed in scrcpy will not always be at the coordinates of 100,100, and there will be an offset with the change of mouse position. Can this be avoided?

title: Handle high-precision scrolling when using notebook touchpads
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I'm investigating yume-chan/ya-webadb#396, a user reported that the scrolling speed of my client implementation, when using notebook touchpads, is super fast.

It turns out that Scrcpy is using the integer scrolling distances (`x`, `y`) from SDL, but mouse wheel events from browsers only contains high-resolution distances (and the event is firing very frequently). It's pretty hard to emulate integer scrolling distances from fractional ones,

In my testing using the native Scrcpy client with a notebook touchpad, the two-fingering scrolling works, but feels clunky.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1330321/177030433-d060a577-0930-4788-a40a-ced0bfb37e9a.mp4

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Since SDL also provides high-resolution scrolling distances (`preciseX` and `preciseY` in [SDL_MouseWheelEvent](https://wiki.libsdl.org/SDL_MouseWheelEvent)), and Android accepts float values in [MotionEvent.PointerCoords.setAxisValue](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/view/MotionEvent.PointerCoords#setAxisValue(int,%20float)), switch Scrcpy to use the high-precision ones. This will make scrolling on notebook touchpad super smooth.

**Additional context**

I tested the idea on https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/compare/master...yume-chan:scrcpy:float-scroll. The result on Windows laptops is good.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1330321/177030443-51cd73d8-c80d-4984-98ff-c63213d3d33a.mp4

author: nikolai-ivanov

title: scrcpy only displays green screen when attempting to use with Samsung Galaxy A03
body:
When connecting the device to my computer and running scrcpy.exe the usual window pops up but the screen is completely green. I can control the device, go full screen, etc but it doesn't show anything else except this weird green screen.

This issue doesn't occur with Vysor or Teamviewer. I have previously connected and used several different android devices on the same set of computers with scrcpy and they worked just fine. I have tried several different cables, tried to connect wirelessly, disabled hardware accelerated tethering, adjusted the screen resolution with `scrcpy.exe -m (different numbers)` before starting scrcpy, replacing the SDL2.dll file with older versions of it self from previous releases of scrcpy, tried older releases of scrcpy and also tested it on different computers.

Something else that I also tried was switching between the different encoders available with `scrcpy --encoder (different encoders)` and each time the scrcpy window still remained green.

Here's an image of the problem: 

![green](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/88605373/177470690-4b08f261-1168-4421-9676-18d1d3ce074b.PNG)


Additional info: scrcpy running on Windows 10 Pro
Android device: Samsung Galaxy A03 (SM-A035F/DS) running on Android 11 (original OS, unmodified rom) 

Any help would be greatly appreciated.

title: Turning the device screen off (-S) leads to dramatic performance loss
body:
- [X] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [X] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: [Windows]
 - scrcpy version: [1.24]
 - installation method: [Windows release]
 - device model: Google Pixel 6 Pro
 - Android version: [12]

**Describe the bug**
Turning the device's screen off leads to a marked performance decrease (at least in games).

------------------------------------------

My args for scrcpy over wifi are as follows:

`scrcpy.exe -b 10M -S -e -m2160`

They are fairly modest settings, but it produced unresolvable FPS lag (15 or worse). I spent a good amount of time looking through the issues forum and saw many people describing reduced FPS, but as far as I could tell, none of it applied to me.

So I decided to test and see whether this was happening over USB. Opened a terminal, ran `scrcpy -d` and voila, there was no lag! I tested around the game menus, which were previously limited to 15fps or worse, and I had full performance. I then noticed that the device screen was still on, so I used the ALT+O hotkey on the streamed window to turn it off (prevent burn-in, reduce power draw, etc). Immediately, the performance tanked back to the previous undesirable levels. Turning the screen back on with ALT+SHIFT+O (or removing the -S arg) seems to send performance back to normal, **_even on wifi_**.

Here is the terminal log showing this in action, I hope it is self-explanitory:

```
scrcpy 1.24 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
C:\Users\XXX\Documents\scrcpy-win64-v1.24\scrcpy-server:... file pushed, 0 skipped. 68.6 MB/s (41159 bytes in 0.001s)
[server] INFO: Device: Google Pixel 6 Pro (Android 12)

INFO: Initial texture: 2160x1000
[server] INFO: Device screen turned off
INFO: FPS counter started
INFO: 16 fps

INFO: 30 fps
INFO: FPS counter stopped
```


If this is intended behaviour, please let me know.

title: Please make .apk for fire tv / amazon android devices that uses USB connection
body:
Thank you for creating scrcpy it works great in a Android to PC/OSX/Linux situation.

If possible please make an Android -> Android port as well Android - > Fire TV ( Amazon Android OS ) port. So far there is no functioning Android -> USB -> Android port of this ( as with the mention OSes ).

All fire TV have the same kind of connection most computer do for the USB stick. Thus should work correctly. However Fire TV /Amazon Android OS has a different video-mode in comparison to regular computers.

If this could be a .apk that works the same way with PC/OSX/Linux it would be perfect. In the past I would scrcpy Android -> PC -> HDMI for my parents as they do not have any high-speed connection for their display. **They have a new Fire TV OS display and it prevents their computer ( HP ) from connecting via HDMI.** The HP has a different frequency or maybe the Fire TV is blocking it ?

I enjoy using scrcpy and recommend it to all of family, friends. classmates, co-workers, and strangers when I can. It would be awesome if I could connect this Android -> Android or Android -> Fire TV/Stick OS /Amazon Android devices 

Thank you for reading and considering making this a .apk release as it is impossible to install xapk in a normal fashion on Fire TV as there is no official .Xapk installer for Fire TV.

This is about plugging in via USB as with the computer but this being a fire tv / Amazon Android OS.

title: batch file (sample attatched) to make it user friendly
body:
- [ ] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

**Describe the solution you'd like**
-
making it more user friendly by having a batch file to do the stuff for you while users only say yes or no

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

**Additional context**
-
some code ive made for myself might be a good headstart

d`@echo off
cd "C:\Users\UserName\Music\scrcpy-win64-v1.24"
:main
echo 1. usb
echo 2. tcp/ip
echo 3. change ip address
echo.

set /p input=enter an option:
set /p bitrate=reduce quality to improve perfomance(y/n(default)) 
set /p aot=Always on top(y(default)/n): 
set /p rec=Turn on recording(y/n(default)): 

set a=--always-on-top
set r=
set bit=

if %aot%==n (set a=)
if %r%==y (set r=--record file.mp4)
if %bitrate%==y (set bit=-m800 --max-fps 15)

if %input%==1 (
scrcpy -d %a% --turn-screen-off --stay-awake --disable-screensaver %r% %bit%
) 

else if %input%==2 (
scrcpy -e %a% -b2M --turn-screen-off --stay-awake --disable-screensaver %r% %bit%
) 

else if %input%==3 (
set /p address=enter new ip address:
scrcpy --tcpip=%address%
)`

title: Push file failed
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows
 - scrcpy version: 1.24
 - installation method: Windows release
 - device model: pixel 3
 - Android version: 11

**Describe the bug**
push file failed
such as file path: C:\Home\Downloads\LANDrop\哦 哦\哈 哈哈.txt

Here is the output of the console: 
```
2022-08-06 14:00:53.464 - logger[INFO] - scrcpy_base_thread.py[44](run) - ScrcpyThread: INFO: Request to push C:\Home\Downloads\LANDrop\哦 哦\哈 哈哈.txt
2022-08-06 14:00:53.469 - logger[INFO] - scrcpy_base_thread.py[44](run) - ScrcpyThread: INFO: Pushing C:\Home\Downloads\LANDrop\哦 哦\哈 哈哈.txt...
2022-08-06 14:00:53.498 - logger[INFO] - scrcpy_base_thread.py[44](run) - ScrcpyThread: C:\Home\Downloads\LANDrop\哦 哦\哈 哈哈.txt: 1 file pushed, 0 skipped. 0.2 MB/s (46 bytes in 0.000s)
2022-08-06 14:00:53.502 - logger[INFO] - scrcpy_base_thread.py[44](run) - ScrcpyThread: adb: error: failed to copy 'C:\Home\Downloads\LANDrop\哦 哦\哈 哈哈.txt' to '/sdcard/Download/.': remote couldn't create file: Is a directory
2022-08-06 14:00:53.517 - logger[INFO] - scrcpy_base_thread.py[44](run) - ScrcpyThread: ERROR: "adb push" returned with value 1
2022-08-06 14:00:53.517 - logger[INFO] - scrcpy_base_thread.py[44](run) - ScrcpyThread: ERROR: Failed to push C:\Home\Downloads\LANDrop\哦 哦\哈 哈哈.txt to /sdcard/Download/
```

I think that executing adb push command needs to set the target file name, which will help to be compatible with Chinese path files with spaces.
for example: 
```
adb push "C:\Home\Downloads\LANDrop\哦 哦\哈 哈哈.txt" "/sdcard/Download/哈 哈哈.txt"
```
top commond should be used instead of bottom commond
```
adb push "C:\Home\Downloads\LANDrop\哦 哦\哈 哈哈.txt" /sdcard/Download/
```

title: adb still running after scrcpy terminates causes rebuilding failure
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows
 - scrcpy version: 1.24
 - installation method: manually built from wsl ubuntu 20.04
 - device model: Xiaomi M2011K2C (Android 11)
 - Android version: 11

**Describe the bug**
When _scrcpy_ is initially built using ```./release.sh``` (in which ```DIST``` is modified to ```/mnt/e/Tools/dist```, which should be irrelevant from my perspective), it works fine. After I run _scrcpy_, terminate it, and then rebuild it, it says

```
rm -rf "/mnt/e/Tools/dist" "build-test" "build-server" \
        "build-win32" "build-win64"
rm: cannot remove '/mnt/e/Tools/dist/scrcpy-win64/adb.exe': Input/output error
rm: cannot remove '/mnt/e/Tools/dist/scrcpy-win64/AdbWinApi.dll': Input/output error
rm: cannot remove '/mnt/e/Tools/dist/scrcpy-win64/AdbWinUsbApi.dll': Input/output error
```
It seems to me that this is because _adb.exe_ is still running, and after I kill it using the task manager in Windows, the rebuilding process works perfectly fine.

So I think _adb.exe_ should be killed on exit.

title: Device screen view bugs out in tiling WMs
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Arch Linux
 - scrcpy version: 1.17.r3.ged130e0-1
 - installation method: AUR
 - device model: Samsung Galaxy S21 Ultra 5G SM-G998B/DS
 - Android version: 12

**Describe the bug**
On a tiling window manager such as i3, where the window size is forced, scrcpy is unable to properly handle when the device rotates. When the device rotates from landscape to portrait, the device screen moves over to the left of the window like this:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/51715529/184021602-234d16a1-7fb0-40ea-929c-364cdf82cc54.png)
And when it goes from portrait to landscape it goes into the bottom left corner like this
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/51715529/184021888-73c243e2-1f3e-4a28-9974-8cb5fc7fa972.png)

In either of these states the device screen does not register mouse clicks properly or at all. This is fixed by toggling the window between full screen and not full screen.

title: Android 13 Release "getDisplayIds" function parameter change, cause scrcpy crash.
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: macOS
 - scrcpy version: 1.24
 - Android version: Android 13

**Describe the bug**
The function `IDisplayManager.getDisplayIds` use in `DisplayManager.getDisplayIds()`.
No longer exist in Android 13, the new function has a bool parameter.
This function cause scrcpy crash.
Please update the scrcpy to fit this issue.

https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/ed84e18b1ae3e51d368f8c7bc88ba4db088e6855/server/src/main/java/com/genymobile/scrcpy/wrappers/DisplayManager.java#L36

[getDisplayIds detail in android code search](https://cs.android.com/android/platform/superproject/+/android-13.0.0_r1:frameworks/base/core/java/android/hardware/display/IDisplayManager.aidl;l=39;bpv=1)
New method detail :
```
    int[] getDisplayIds(boolean includeDisabled);
```

title: Screen freeze on Redmi Note 10 Pro (problem is not in ADB/not in the phone)
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).
 Found this one but seems not a related problem despite same symptom: https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues/3442
This one also but not sure also: https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues/3051

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows 11 Pro
 - scrcpy version: 1.24
 - installation method: Windows release...
 - device model: Redmi Note 10 Pro Max
 - Android version: Android 12 with MIUI 13.0.5

**Describe the bug**
I've got the `[server] ERROR: Encoding error: java.lang.IllegalStateException: null` described in issue #3051. Using `--encoder 'OMX.google.h264.encoder'` solve the issue, but screen capture freeze on the first frame. Remote control is functionnal after granting it from developer options settings on the phone.

The problem doesn't happens when I use ADB and FFPLAY to stream the screen to my PC with the following command-line:
```
adb exec-out screenrecord --size 1280x720 --output-format=h264 - | "C:\Program Files\ffmpeg\bin\ffplay.exe" -framerate 30 -probesize 32 -sync video -
```
As ADB is functionnal with ffplay, I conclude that screencast using ADB is correct and I think that this is a bug from `scrcpy`. Could anyone here help with this issue?

title: Android 12: Server connection failed | ART APEX data files are untrusted
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Fedora 36
 - scrcpy version: 1.24
 - installation method: manual build from `master` with prebuilt server
 - device model: Motorola G6 (`ali`)
 - Android version: 12.1

**Bug description**
Ever since my custom ROM got updated to Android 12.1 (LineageOS 19.1), `scrcpy` no longer works - at all. Using a manual build from `master` with prebuilt server, I receive the following console and `adb` output (I have redacted the device's serial number).

Console output:
```
$ scrcpy  -Vdebug
scrcpy 1.24 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
DEBUG: ADB device found:
DEBUG:     -->   (usb)  ZY32******                      device  moto_g_6_
DEBUG: Device serial: ZY32******
DEBUG: Using server: /usr/local/share/scrcpy/scrcpy-server
/usr/local/share/scrcpy/scrcpy-server: 1 file pushed, 0 skipped. 184.7 MB/s (41159 bytes in 0.000s)
DEBUG: Interrupting socket
DEBUG: Server disconnected
DEBUG: Server terminated
ERROR: Server connection failed
```
`adb logcat ` output:
```
08-24 15:13:43.962 16254 16254 D AndroidRuntime: >>>>>> START com.android.internal.os.RuntimeInit uid 0 <<<<<<
08-24 15:13:43.990 16254 16254 I AndroidRuntime: Using default boot image
08-24 15:13:43.990 16254 16254 I AndroidRuntime: Leaving lock profiling enabled
08-24 15:13:44.006 16254 16254 W app_process: ART APEX data files are untrusted.
08-24 15:13:44.007 16254 16254 E app_process: Could not open boot oat file for extracting boot class path: File /apex/com.android.art/javalib/boot.art:/system/framework/boot-framework.art!/system/etc/arm64/boot-image.vdex does not exist.
08-24 15:13:44.007 16254 16254 E AndroidRuntime: JNI_CreateJavaVM failed
```
I build the custom ROM myself, so any simple change on that side should be possible.

How can I help fix this issue?

Thanks,
FWieP

title: Lagging and freezing in display window
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows 11
 - scrcpy version: 1.24
 - installation method: Windows release
 - device model: Moto G(6) Play
 - Android version: 9

I am having some problems with lag and dropped frames when running scrcpy to capture in OBS. My phone does not seem to be lagging in the game when scrcpy is active, but the window that displays it on my computer which I am recording with OBS keeps freezing and lagging hugely.

This is not a constant problem - usually everything works fine - but when it starts randomly during recording the only consistent solution I have is to completely restart my phone. I am using USB connection so the problem is probably with the performance of my phone/PC rather than the connection.

Does scrcpy run any processes on my phone's CPU, or is it all on the computer? If I know what device the lag originated on, that might help me to fix this problem.

I am running [scrcpy -d --max-fps 30 -b 2M -m 1280] in the command prompt window, to get 30fps 720p resolution.

Another option would be for it to be logged to a file.

title: External monitor mode for otg mode (Universal control mode).
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When the otg mode is enabled on windows,
You can enter the phone through the otg window.
But there is no gui method to exit the phone screen(you have to alt- tab out of the window).
It would be nice if there was a gui way to exit the phone screen without using the keyboard

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Maybe there could be an external monitor mode in which the mouse behaves in a similar way to how it would behave if an external monitor was connected.

If the mouse exits from a designated edge of the screen, it enters the phone.
And if exits from the corresponding edge of the phone screen, it re-enters the desktop screen.

Similar to how universal control on mac and ipad works.

title: Server fails when built with SDK < 33 on macOS
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - macOS 10.15.7 (Catalina)
 - 1.24
 - manual build
 - Sony Xperia G8441
 - Android 9

**Problem**
When building from source, I keep getting
```
[server] INFO: Device: Sony G8441 (Android 9)
[server] ERROR: Exception on thread Thread[main,5,main]
java.lang.AssertionError: java.lang.NullPointerException: 
 Attempt to invoke virtual method
 'java.lang.reflect.Method java.lang.Class.getMethod(java.lang.String, java.lang.Class[])'
 on a null object reference
        at com.genymobile.scrcpy.wrappers.WindowManager.registerRotationWatcher(WindowManager.java:108)
        at com.genymobile.scrcpy.Device.<init>(Device.java:85)
        at com.genymobile.scrcpy.Server.scrcpy(Server.java:65)
        at com.genymobile.scrcpy.Server.main(Server.java:335)
        at com.android.internal.os.RuntimeInit.nativeFinishInit(Native Method)
        at com.android.internal.os.RuntimeInit.main(RuntimeInit.java:340)
Caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException:
 Attempt to invoke virtual method
 'java.lang.reflect.Method java.lang.Class.getMethod(java.lang.String, java.lang.Class[])'
 on a null object reference
        at com.genymobile.scrcpy.wrappers.WindowManager.registerRotationWatcher(WindowManager.java:102)
        ... 5 more
2022-09-02 13:12:05.549 scrcpy[48321:5695314] DEBUG: Interrupting socket
2022-09-02 13:12:05.549 scrcpy[48321:5695314] DEBUG: Server disconnected
2022-09-02 13:12:05.549 scrcpy[48321:5695314] DEBUG: Server terminated
```

**Fix**
Changing `PLATFORM` and `BUILD_TOOLS` in `server/build_without_gradle.sh` to version 33.

**Notes**
This has been an issue for "quite a while". (I've had to use the prebuilt server.)
Thought I could bisect to find where this issue appeared, but found that it was already present in v1.23, and couldn't build v1.22, because an older `ffmpeg` was expected.
