title: Chocolatey Only Installs V1.17 and with Traffmonetizer
body:
- [ ] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [ ] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: [e.g. Debian, Windows, macOS...]  WINDOWS 10
 - scrcpy version: [e.g. 1.12.1]   1.17
 - installation method: [e.g. manual build, apt, snap, brew, Windows release...]  Installed via Chocolatey in Powershell.  
 - device model:  Nvidia TV Shield PRO
 - Android version: [e.g. 10]  Android TV Latest

**Describe the bug**
It looks like SCRCPY installs a Monetizer called Traffmonetizer and interferes with the operation of the TV Shield.

Whilst watching Netflix of Amazon Prime or running Emby, the screen will flash and go white and the main home screen appears, flash white again a couple of times again and display the Traffmonetizer logo. I was able to to take a short video of this and then grab a capture of the logo which allowed me to trace the problem.  Sometimes the screen will disappear and I am returned to what I am watching, other times the app will abort and I remain on the home screen.
This is an example from using the Youtube app.
https://youtu.be/SwitH82Qsjw

```
Please paste terminal output in a code block.
This is the output from 'installing' via Chocolatey:
`
PS C:\WINDOWS\system32> choco install scrcpy
Chocolatey v0.10.15
Installing the following packages:
scrcpy
By installing you accept licenses for the packages.
scrcpy v1.17 already installed.
 Use --force to reinstall, specify a version to install, or try upgrade.

Chocolatey installed 0/1 packages.
 See the log for details (C:\ProgramData\chocolatey\logs\chocolatey.log).

Warnings:
 - scrcpy - scrcpy v1.17 already installed.
 Use --force to reinstall, specify a version to install, or try upgrade.
PS C:\WINDOWS\system32>
[chocolatey.log](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/files/9595525/chocolatey.log)
`

```

Please do not post screenshots of your terminal, just post the content as text instead.
See youtube video of the problem with the Traffmonetizer that got pushed onto the Shield with Scrcpy.  I know this is the culprit because I did a factory reset on the Shield to remove the problem assuming it was in a store app, but after the reset I did not instrall any apps off the store and only re-implemented the Developer mode, Networking debugging and installed Scrcpy via ADB.

author: yangfl

title: Check `$ANDROID_HOME` environment variable for the android data files
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
No. I'm an Arch Linux user, and despite my sedentary and unsanitary lifestyle, I would like to keep my home directory clean. It only has 16 folders and 7 files in its root. One of the folders in the said 16 folders is the `.android`. Upon further inspection, this file is generated by this program. Now, I've set `$ANDROID_HOME` to `"$XDG_DATA_HOME"/android`, and moved `.android` to said directory as per xdg-ninja's suggestion. However, it still appears.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Make this program check `$ANDROID_HOME` environment variable for the android data files, and use it. 

If the program did not see said environment variable, generate one on the home directory, or much preferably, at `"$XDG_DATA_HOME"/android` if set.

[XDG Base Directory](https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/XDG_Base_Directory) is a specification that defines where files should go. In this specification, it also defines where programs would put the data files, called `XDG_DATA_HOME`, which defaults to `$HOME/.local/share`.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
None, as this program generates `.android`, and I do not see an option that makes this program read android data files from another directory.

**Rationale**
Standardization, and making `$HOME` less cluttered.

**Additional context**
OS: Linux (Arch distribution)
Kernel: 5.19.11-arch1-1

**Reference**
- https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/XDG_Base_Directory
- https://specifications.freedesktop.org/basedir-spec/basedir-spec-latest.html

title: Mirrored screen infinite loop
body:
- [ X ] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [ X ] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: macOS Monterey 
 - scrcpy version: 1.24
 - installation method: brew
 - device model: LG G4 H810
 - Android version: 6.0

**Describe the bug**
After starting scrcpy, screen is mirrored to Mac with no issues for a few minutes.  After that, the screen suddenly stops being transmitted and on the Mac, the last half second or so before transmission ceased repeats in an infinite loop, even though the display on the Android device is fine.  Rotating the device will restore the screen image showing up correctly on the Mac.  No errors seen on the console.

```
scrcpy 1.24 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>

2022-10-26 15:03:54.203 scrcpy[32654:1039335] INFO: New texture: 2560x1440

```

body:

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Unable to control the phone with broken screen and adb turned off.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add the ability to create input event macros that you save to a file. This will allow you to create a macro on your phone with adb enabled. Then you can run this macro on the phone without adb enabled to run this option. 

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Ready preset with the ability to play scripts.

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

  google pixel 6 pro, grapheneOS (android13)
  asus nexus7, android 6
  samsung SM-T510, android 11

Primary work on pixel, other devices used to make sure problem is not os/device specific

**Describe the bug**
```
scrcpy --max-fps=30  --forward-all-clicks --verbosity=verbose --display=2
```
When using scrcpy with aFreeRDP, the right and middle mouse button don't work as expected.
I don't use the hid (AOA). In case of pixel, mouse pointer appears on the primary display. In case of samsung tab, it shows up on the extra display. I have not found a way to control it.

**Investigation**
Initially I assumed that the problem is in the aFreeRDP and started by dumping all motion events delivered to dispatchTouchEvent and dispatchGenericMotionEvent.
The first impression: ACTION_UP is missing for right and middle click. The event is not delivered. But perhaps it is the app itself.
So, I took a physical mouse and captured events. Than captured events when delivered from scrcpy. 

Right click events from physical mouse as seen by the application, the attributes which are always the same are in const section.
```
dispatchTouchEvent:         ACTION_DOWN,           actionButton=0, id[0]=0, x[0]=0.0, y[0]=2257.9397,                buttonState=BUTTON_SECONDARY, eventTime=11857929, ventId=507666466 }
dispatchGenericMotionEvent: ACTION_BUTTON_PRESS,   actionButton=BUTTON_SECONDARY, id[0]=0, x[0]=0.0, y[0]=2257.9397, buttonState=BUTTON_SECONDARY, eventTime=11857929, ventId=608438892 }
dispatchGenericMotionEvent: ACTION_BUTTON_RELEASE, actionButton=BUTTON_SECONDARY, id[0]=0, x[0]=0.0, y[0]=2257.9397, buttonState=0,                eventTime=11858273, ventId=612919901 }
dispatchTouchEvent:         ACTION_UP,             actionButton=0, id[0]=0, x[0]=0.0, y[0]=2257.9397,                buttonState=0,                eventTime=11858273, ventId=76737027 }
dispatchGenericMotionEvent: ACTION_HOVER_MOVE,     actionButton=0, id[0]=0, x[0]=0.0, y[0]=2257.9397,                buttonState=0,                eventTime=11858273, ventId=655777164 }

const:
toolType[0]=TOOL_TYPE_MOUSE,
classification=NONE, metaState=0, flags=0x0, edgeFlags=0x0, pointerCount=1, historySize=0,
downTime=11857929, deviceId=6, source=0x2002, displayId=0,

```

Right click events when delivered from scrcpy, the attributes which are always the same are in const section.
In addition log contains scrcpy events just before injection point (Controller.java, injectTouch)
```
scrcpy  injectEvent: ACTION_DOWN, id[0]=0, x[0]=710.0, y[0]=1668.0, buttonState=BUTTON_SECONDARY, eventTime=14278106, deviceId=0,  source=0x2002, displayId=0, eventId=-1002372252 }
FreeRDP dispatchTou: ACTION_DOWN, id[0]=0, x[0]=710.0, y[0]=1668.0, buttonState=BUTTON_SECONDARY, eventTime=14278106, deviceId=-1, source=0x2002, displayId=0, eventId=-1002372252 }
scrcpy  injectEvent: ACTION_UP,   id[0]=0, x[0]=710.0, y[0]=1668.0, buttonState=0,                eventTime=14278149, deviceId=0,  source=0x1002, displayId=0, eventId=-687213207 }

const:
toolType[0]=TOOL_TYPE_FINGER,
actionButton=0,
classification=NONE, metaState=0, flags=0x0, edgeFlags=0x0, pointerCount=1, historySize=0,
downTime=14278106,
```

The highlights are:
- missing touch ACTION_UP in the aFreeRDP, but can see the injected touch event in server
- missing motion events (generic motion events) which is probably not the real problem, but would be nice for consistency. Not sure about the performance, the hoover is send on each move.
- inconsistent deviceId, probably not a problem
- toolType is FINGER instead of MOUSE, again not a deal breaker
- wrong source (FINGER instead of MOUSE) in ACTION_UP event injected by the scrcpy

When I hard-code source to 0x2002 in injectTouch, the ACTION_UP is delivered to the application.
But this is just to prove why android is swallowing the event.

title: scrcpy --tcpip doesn't setup a connection correctly when wireless debugging runs on a different port than 5555
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: MacOS (but it's irrelevant)
 - scrcpy version: 1.24
 - installation method: brew
 - device model: OnePlus LE2125 (OnePlus 9 Pro)
 - Android version: 11

**Describe the bug**

Phone connected via USB, visible by ADB:

```
% adb devices
List of devices attached
8253bf5d	device
```

Running `scrcpy --tcpip` to do the heavy lifting ("It will automatically find the device IP address, enable TCP/IP mode, then connect to the device before starting") for me:

```
% scrcpy --tcpip
scrcpy 1.24 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
2022-11-22 16:27:00.398 scrcpy[42003:780512] INFO: Switching device 8253bf5d to TCP/IP...
2022-11-22 16:27:00.492 scrcpy[42003:780512] INFO: Connecting to 172.20.167.222:5555...
failed to connect to '172.20.167.222:5555': Connection refused
2022-11-22 16:27:01.236 scrcpy[42003:780512] ERROR: Could not connect to 172.20.167.222:5555
2022-11-22 16:27:01.237 scrcpy[42003:780511] ERROR: Server connection failed
```

... But it won't succeed because wireless debugging runs on a different port. I had to `adb tcpip 5555` to change wireless debugging port to 5555 and only then would `scrcpy --tcpip` succeed.

# Feature request

```diff
- It will automatically find the device IP address, enable TCP/IP mode, then connect to the device before starting
+ It will automatically find the device IP address, enable TCP/IP mode, find debugging port, then connect to the device before starting
```

title: solution to "adb remote secure_mkdirs failed: Permission denied"
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows
 - scrcpy version: 1.24
 - installation method: Windows release
 - device model: Redmi K50
 - Android version: 12

**Describe the bug**
When you root your phone with Magisk, looks like Magisk changed some settings and causing
`adbd cannot run as root in production builds` when you type `adb root`, and causing `adb remote secure_mkdirs failed: Permission denied` when you executing the bat.
This might not be a fixable bug, but I still want to post my solution due to lots of people facing this problem too, since [adbd insecure](https://forum.xda-developers.com/t/2014-11-10-root-adbd-insecure-v2-00.1687590/) and [adb_root](https://github.com/evdenis/adb_root) won't work on Android 12.

1. adb shell
2. su
3. magisk resetprop ro.debuggable 1
4. stop;start;

After phone restarting, you should be able to use scrcpy properly.
Note: You might need to do it again if you reboot your phone.

body:

 - scrcpy version: 1.24
 - installation method: manual build
 - device model: OnePlus 3
 - Android version: 11 (crDroid 7.19)

**Describe the bug**

I am using scrcpy on my Raspberry Pi to mirror my phone screen using this script :
```
#!/bin/bash
adb push /usr/local/share/scrcpy/scrcpy-server /data/local/tmp/scrcpy-server.jar
adb forward tcp:1234 localabstract:scrcpy
adb shell CLASSPATH=/data/local/tmp/scrcpy-server.jar app_process / com.genymobile.scrcpy.Server 1.24 raw_video_stream=true tunnel_forward=true control=false &
omxplayer --no-keys --no-osd --fps 60  --advanced=0 --loop --timeout 0 --lavfdopts probesize:250000 --video_queue 1 --avdict rtsp_transport:tcp tcp://localhost:1234 &
exit 0
```
Is there a way to use the '--turn-screen-off' and '--stay-awake' options in this case? My phone display is broken and is causing lags if these options are not used.

title: Killing the main scrcpy process doesn't terminate the scrcpy window
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: [Windows and macOS]
 - scrcpy version: [1.24, 1.25]
 - installation method: [Windows release, brew]
 - device model: Not specific to any device

**Describe the bug**

On Mac - 

With the commit 0426708 , the command for starting scrcpy-server is run in a separate thread. Killing the main process with a SIGTERM (`kill <pid>`) only kills the main process and leaves the scrcpy window running. If SIGKILL is used instead (`kill -9 <pid>`), the scrcpy window does close but, the server thread seems to be running as seen by the `ps` output. This suggests to me that the cleanup is probably not happening as expected.

On Windows -

Haven't done much digging for windows but killing the initial scrcpy process from task manager, leaves the scrcpy window open and ofcourse, scrcpy-server thread is also running.

This issue is relevant for me because I am calling the scrcpy executable from another process, and I can only access the initial scrcpy process and send a SIGKILL to it. So, I am either not able to close scrcpy (on windows) or scrcpy-server cleanup is not finished (on mac). I have not yet tested on a Linux system but I suspect the issue will be similar to the one observed on Mac

title: sc_adb_execute may be missing Error Handling
body:

 - [ √] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - scrcpy version: [1.25]

**Describe the bug**
The function sc_adb_execute_p in adb.c checks whether its return value is equal to SC_PROCESS_NONE every time it is called. However, the function sc_adb_execute calls sc_adb_execute_p directly, but all calls to the sc_adb_execute function in adb.c do not check whether their return value is equal to SC_PROCESS_NONE. Is this a potential Error Handling bug?

title: Support mouse wheel for scroll (perhaps with cli option to enable)
body:
- [X] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

I find the control-drag for screen zoom to be awkward and unintuitive on a desktop PC.  It's not the most terrible way to emulate pinch-to-zoom on a mobile device, but if you're trying to emulate mobile-device human interface features on a desktop PC with a keyboard and mouse, your UI metaphor is probably wrong.  Microsoft made that mistake with Windows 8, "Make everything act like a mobile device", and Windows 8 tanked.  (Deservedly.)

**Describe the solution you'd like**

How difficult would it be to add functionality — perhaps enabled by a command-line or configuration option, so as not to unnecessarily break existing behaviors — to use, say, Ctrl + mousewheel to zoom in and out?  The vast majority of PC desktop applications and games that use zoom use the mouse wheel to zoom.  It would be a much more familiar metaphor for the environment.

body:
- [X] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).

```
scrcpy 1.24 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
Dependencies (compiled / linked):
    - SDL: 2.0.22 / 2.0.22
    - libavcodec: 59.18.100 / 59.18.100
    - libavformat: 59.16.100 / 59.16.100

```

title: Frames capped at 20fps/12fps
body:
Hi 

I usually have this problem where, after sometime of running the program smoothly, i run into this bug where the frames either cap at 20fps or 12fps. It'll only go lower and it'll be so choppy it's freaking annoying. My phone is fast (Galaxy S20FE 5G) and my laptop used to run it smoothly. It even overshot sometimes, above 60fps causing lag which is actually quite ironic (not trying to be rude here). I really don't know what could cause this as it has been running fine and just suddenly it just caps.

Sorry for how I'm sounding. By the way this program is a lifesaver. I've literally used it on all my phones, even friends phones. My friends have asked me how I found it. It's a real game changer. Thanks a lot!

title: Finally got Scrcpy to work on wireless, but no longer able to transfer files over
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Linux Mint 20.2 (Debian/Ubuntu based)
 - scrcpy version: 1.25
 - installation method: manual build
 - device model: LG K10 (2017, MTK-based)
 - Android version: 7.0 Nougat

**Describe the bug**
Unfortunately on this version of Android and on this phone of mine, connecting to my phone isn't just a simple matter of launching Scrcpy on my laptop and get it to find my phone and pronto, it works. No, my phone always denies access via port 5555, forcing me to root it, then I launch a terminal-emulator and I do `setprop service.adb.tcp.port <portnumber>` and then I do `adbd restart`on my phone and then I FINALLY get to connect to it. In other words, only via root now, and no, I have no idea why my phone has become a major pain to connect to 5555. But that's the workaround.
And get this, if I connect it to my laptop via usb and don't do any of the steps mentioned above, I can have access to my microsd card and write to it.
However, when doing those steps above (going wireless with scrcpy), I'm no longer able to write to my sdcard directory anymore, which defeats the purpose of having scrcpy in order to transfer files from my laptop to my phone while it's changing next to a wall.
Maybe this has to do with the fact that to define a new port for adb to listen to, I have to be a superuser and that's screwed user access to microsd files..? I used to be able to do this before, but now it spits a permission denied error:
```
adb shell
mlv5:/ $ ls storage/244F-02CD/                                                          
ls: storage/244F-02CD//.android_secure: Permission denied
```
Which is funny because if I do ls -l, it outputs:
```
$ ls -l storage/244F-02CD/                                                     
ls: storage/244F-02CD//.android_secure: Permission denied
total 3840
drwxr-xr-x 1 root everybody 131072 2019-06-21 17:22 AMAZON
drwxr-xr-x 1 root everybody 131072 2019-05-10 18:14 AZRecorder
drwxr-x--- 1 root everybody 131072 2020-11-13 03:35 Android
drwxr-xr-x 1 root everybody 131072 2022-06-17 09:59 Capture+
drwxr-xr-x 1 root everybody 131072 2021-06-16 01:22 DCIM
drwxr-xr-x 1 root everybody 131072 2021-08-01 09:42 Documents

```
What exactly do I need to change in order to be able to write to my sdcard again? I'm running out of ideas.

Thanks in advance.

author: hosamasd

title: Display an android image using iOS
body: - [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist. **Describe the solution you'd like** A clear and concise description of what you want to happen. ## Preface Hi, first of all thank you for your work on scrcpy, it's a great project, and the amount of code in the project has really paid off Long ago, I put forward in the issue to scrcpy transplantation to the Android idea, https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues/1235, soon after that I will succeed For porting, I read almost the entire scrcpy client + server code First of all, we get ffmpeg related arm64 so library through cross-compilation, and integrate SDL2 into Android through jni through some SDL2 articles It looks something like this. Then to port the scrcpy client code to Android completely, you need to supplement config.h and write a cmakefilelist file that looks something like this In the work is completed, I will try to build a complete App, called Uncon software, there is an introduction video at https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV1Sd4y1v7bv/?spm_id_from=333.999.0.0 You can download the software to try it out (sorry again, there is no support for internationalization at the moment). https://drive.google.com/file/d/1I3nj02w8nxehAak9TMTHLuSD4JqCSLMn/view?usp=sharing > If the video is not accessible and you are interested, you can contact me and I will upload it to YouTube and other ways > > Many domestic manufacturers, including Huawei and Xiaomi, are doing some ecological interconnection, including remote control within the LAN, which is one of the reasons for the birth of this software There are both soft decoding and hard decoding. After porting the soft decoding part (scrcpy source code), I found that it didn't work well on Android devices and started using the Scrcpy-Server custom protocol. I wrote a hard decoding code using Android's native API, and it did perform better than soft decoding ## Thoughts Recently I came up with the idea of **having iOS display an Android screen image and be able to control it** In principle, this works -- iOS has a native adb library, and even if it didn't, it wouldn't be difficult for a web-based library to rewrite an adb library that includes key apis This part I also practice, there is a blog, https://juejin.cn/post/7034799230086545445, Chinese... Based on the above conditions, iOS can push scrcpy-server to an Android device and then call app_process to run servre I had the Flutter/Android stack, but didn't know anything about iOS, so the rest of it was a hassle for me Based on previous experience with Android, it could go one of two ways 1. Try porting ffmpeg and sdl2 to iOS and then porting all of scrcpy client code to iOS 2. Use iOS native api to decode the video in scrcpy-server 3. Is it possible to push the video spits out by scrcpy (rtmp), so that it can be played by the existing video player of iOS, only need to realize the customization of screen control. The third way is when I read an idea from a blog https://www.jianshu.com/p/6dde380d9b1e After releasing this issue, I will try some things in my spare time, and I will synchronize to this issue if there is any progress. Any ideas, thank you

author: unique-EJ

title: Rename the new `uid` option
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

`dev` branch has a new option called `uid`, for differentiating multiple running Scrcpy instances. IMO it should not be called `uid`, because `uid` is a common Linux concept, which might be useful in other cases.

For example, with this little change, I can create a secure display on rooted Android 12+ devices (tested in an emulator):

https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/compare/dev...yume-chan:scrcpy:feat/secure-display?expand=1

Unrooted:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1330321/218240859-4236a4a3-cecf-4c92-8384-e91cecdf2075.png)

Rooted:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1330321/218240870-5f9e2f48-a4be-4a02-b8e9-5b62dad1ffd2.png)

Use `setUid(2000)` instead of `1000` can fix issues like #1606

So, the new option should be renamed, the new name can be `instance_id` or other more descriptive names.

title: Accented letters not getting sent over from PC keyboard
body:
- [X] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [X] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows
 - scrcpy version: 1.25
 - installation method: Windows release
 - device model: Motorola Moto G51 5G (XT2171-2)
 - Android version: 12

**Describe the bug**
When mirroring my phone and typing through my PC keyboard, some accented characters will not get sent through to the phone.
Console prints out:
```[server] WARN: Could not inject char <character key>```
Examples include Croatian letters
 - Š/š (u+0160/u+161)
 - Đ/đ (u+0110 / u+111)
 - Č/č (u+010c/u+010d)
 - Ž/ž (u+017d/u+017e)
Some other accented characters are sent over without issues (Ć/ć).

Same issue happens when I try writing in Serbian Cyrillic script, none of the letters get sent through, I get a console log, and a Latin variant of the letter is sent instead. E.g. if I type "Hi" as "Хи", I get "Hi" in the input box and the console prints 
```
[server] WARN: Could not inject char u+0425
[server] WARN: Could not inject char u+0438
```
Same issue happens with Danish letters Å (u+00c5), å (u+00e5, Æ(u+00c6),  æ (u+00e6), Ø (u+00d8), ø (u+00f8), and probably other scripts too.

Thank you for fixing this!

title: JPEG frame stream from the server
body:
- [x ] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Currently I am only using the server code to get the h264 video stream and sending the video frames to remote. One of the problem with the h264 is that when the network is not stable, especially when it has packet loss, the video will be laggy and freezing. Because it has to wait for the next IDR frame to render on the receiver side.

Do we support other formats like JPEG frames from the server? It would be super helpful if the server can directly product a stream of JPEG frames. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
We can set the format/codec we want when we start the server. 

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Or is there any easy way to transform the h264 frame stream to JPEG on the server side.

title: Add time-limit argument
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

Related but not solving my issue:
* https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues/1032
* https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues/818
* https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues/1032

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I am trying to automate the acquisition of videos from an android phone which can last for hours.
I know how long the recording should be, so something like `adb screenrecord --time-limit` would be great (see [here](https://adbshell.com/commands/adb-shell-screenrecord))

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Something similar to `adb screenrecord --time-limit=S <file>` would be great (see [here](https://adbshell.com/commands/adb-shell-screenrecord)), where `S` is the amount of time in seconds of the desired recording duration.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
My automation system is only working on windows, unfortunately.
I tried interrupting the scrcpy process after a fixed amount of time in multiple ways but it is either not stopping or produces a corrupted video file.

Tests:
* `start /B scrcpy -Nr test.mp4 && timeout /t 5 /nobreak >nul && taskkill /im scrcpy.exe /f`: corrupted file
* Python script 
```python
import subprocess, signal, time

proc = subprocess.Popen(["scrcpy", "-Nr", "test.mp4"],
                        creationflags=subprocess.CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP)
print("sleeping")
time.sleep(5)
print("terminating")
proc.terminate() # also tried proc.send_signal(signal.CTRL_C_EVENT) and proc.send_signal(signal.CTRL_BREAK_EVENT)
```
  * Results: corrupted file with `terminate()` and `CTRL_BREAK_EVENT`, process not terminating with `CTR_C_EVENT`. In any case, no useful output file
* While running scrcpy, send `adb shell pkill <adb process pid>` (see https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues/818#issuecomment-535931801). Nothing happens.

title: Wireless ADB gives "NoSuchMethodException", and a few minutes later "Warn: Device disconnected"
body:
- [x ] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x ] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows 11
 - scrcpy version: 1.22
 - installation method: Windows release
 - device model: Pixel 7
 - Android version: 13

**Describe the bug**
When connecting ADB over Wifi, scrcpy will usually connect for a few minutes, then disconnect with the `WARN: Device disconnected` message. However, the console outputs some lines with errors before that, so I don't know if there's anything I need to change to make it run better and hopefully stop crashing? I haven't been able to capture logcat for it, but here is the full CMD output:

```
C:\Program Files\SCRCPY\scrcpy-server: 1 file pushed, 0 skipped. 3.4 MB/s (40955 bytes in 0.012s)
[server] INFO: Device: Google Pixel 7 (Android 13)
[server] ERROR: Could not invoke method
java.lang.NoSuchMethodException: android.content.IClipboard$Stub$Proxy.addPrimaryClipChangedListener [interface android.content.IOnPrimaryClipChangedListener, class java.lang.String, int]
        at java.lang.Class.getMethod(Class.java:2103)
        at java.lang.Class.getMethod(Class.java:1724)
        at com.genymobile.scrcpy.wrappers.ClipboardManager.getAddPrimaryClipChangedListener(ClipboardManager.java:103)
        at com.genymobile.scrcpy.wrappers.ClipboardManager.addPrimaryClipChangedListener(ClipboardManager.java:111)
        at com.genymobile.scrcpy.Device.<init>(Device.java:103)
        at com.genymobile.scrcpy.Server.scrcpy(Server.java:64)
        at com.genymobile.scrcpy.Server.main(Server.java:326)
        at com.android.internal.os.RuntimeInit.nativeFinishInit(Native Method)
        at com.android.internal.os.RuntimeInit.main(RuntimeInit.java:355)
INFO: Renderer: direct3d
INFO: Initial texture: 1080x2400
WARN: Device disconnected
```

Is there an issue that I can address regarding the NoSuchMethodException?
author: e-t-l

title: PLEASE UPDATE CHOCOLATEY PACKAGE (choco installs v1.24. not 1.25)
body:
- [x ] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x ] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows 11
 - scrcpy version: 1.24 (cannot upgrade to 1.25)
 - installation method: choco
 - device model: n/a
 - Android version: n/a

**Describe the bug**
Choco only installs up to v1.24. Attempting to specify with the version flag, i.e. `choco install scrcpy --version 1.25`, returns an error that no such file exists.

title: Using ScreenEncoder separately
body:
- [ ] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I want to use the ScreenEncoder file separately, to capture and encode the screen video stream in an Android project, transmit it through our own service, and display it. However, I don't know how to fill in these parameters, and I'm not sure if I can do it.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I want to use the screen capturing and encoding feature inside ScreenEncoder separately, and obtain the output stream.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I have tried using ScreenEncoder separately, but I couldn't find where some of the default parameters are located.

**Additional context**
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

title: Scrcpy
body:
E aí gente tô com umas dúvidas em relação a ferramenta scrcpy,quero usar a webcam  v4l2loopback via obs Studio pra espelhar no Android só que não sei exatamente como fazer isso! De fato o que ocorre e que instalei e configurei certo o scrcpy e o obs-studio até então início o obs-studio e seleciono "fontes" e coloco a opção "gravador de vídeo v4l2l" e seleciono a minha webcam padrão do computador /video0,e no final clico na opção "start virtual webcam",e após fazer isso vou na opção de iniciar o scrcpy já com a câmera ativada na outra parte,o obs Studio está no /dev/video2,até então eu início com o comando scrcpy --v4l2-sink=/dev/video2,só que quando abro a câmera normal do Android já com a tela espelhada no computador,não aparece a webcam que eu escolhi,apenas a câmera normal do celular,alguém aí tem sugestões e idéias e até mesmo se estou fazendo algo errado me corrigir,aceito críticas! Sistema operacional utilizado:Linux fedora 37 👍

And then we have some doubts regarding the scrcpy tool, I want to use the v4l2loopback webcam via obs Studio to mirror on Android, but I don't know exactly how to do that! In fact what happens is that I installed and configured scrcpy and obs-studio correctly until then I start obs-studio and select "sources" and put the option "v4l2l video recorder" and select my computer's default webcam / video0 , and at the end I click on the "start virtual webcam" option, and after doing that I go to the option to start scrcpy with the camera already activated in the other part, obs Studio is in /dev/video2, until then I start with the command scrcpy --v4l2-sink=/dev/video2, but when I open the normal Android camera with the screen mirrored on the computer, the webcam I chose does not appear, just the normal cell phone camera, does anyone have suggestions and ideas and even if I'm doing something wrong correct me, I accept criticism! Operating system used: Linux fedora 37 👍

author: rehack

title: Build failure on Fedora 36, AV_CH_LAYOUT_STEREO undefined.
body:
scrcpy 2.0 doesn't build on Fedora 36, ffmpeg 5.0.2 – probably because of https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/commit/d66b0b3dccc118600b83988173e6631ad661df51.

Including `libavutil/channel_layout.h` in `demuxer.c` seems to fix the issue.

It builds fine on later Fedora versions without any changes.

author: arunar101

title: Audio as a system input
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I want to use `scrcpy` as an input to OBS on Windows. The audio currently is "Desktop Audio" and hence captures sound from all apps. Is it possible to have the audio as a capture device so that I can input it cleanly to OBS?

**Describe the solution you'd like**
An option to create the audio stream from `scrcpy` as a separate input device (aka audio capture device in OBS)

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Using `scrcpy` as a window capture / game capture source in OBS, but audio is not just for the device but rather from all running applications.

**Additional context**
Windows is my primary use-case - it would be great to have this in Linux too.

body:

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows11 Professional x64, antivirus software turned off
 - scrcpy version: scrcpy-win64-v2.0
 - installation method: Windows release
 - device model: Xiaomi CC9e in China
 - Android version:  Android 10

**Describe the bug**
1. Scrcpy location: "D:\scrcpy-win64-v2.0"

2. ADB debugging enabled
``` 
D:\scrcpy-win64-v2.0>adb devices
* daemon not running; starting now at tcp:5037
* daemon started successfully
List of devices attached
3ed52b1d0f6b    device
```

```
D:\scrcpy-win64-v2.0>scrcpy
scrcpy 2.0 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
adb: error: stat failed when trying to push to /data/local/tmp/scrcpy-server.jar: Permission denied
ERROR: "adb push" returned with value 1
ERROR: Server connection failed
```

6. I try to copy "scrcpy server" to "/data/local/tmp/scrcpy server. jar" and chmod775

Still failed to cast the screen！！！

title: "robotic" and glitchy sound
body:
- [ ] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [ ] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows 10 Professional x64
 - scrcpy version: 2.0
 - installation method: Windows release
 - device model: Realme GT 5G
 - Android version: 12

**Describe the bug**
I've tried changing audio buffer to higher values (video buffering too), have also changed audio bitrate and so on, but it always come out as a "robotic" sound with many "clicks" and "impurities" as well (I'm trying my best to describe it, bear with me, please). So, when I decided I was going to place an issue about this, of course I recorded a minuscule sample video to showcase it. I was displeasedly surprised after playing it with perfect sound, not "robotic" or glitchy at all, just smooth audio playback. That's why I didn't consider uploading that sample.

So, I've hunted my Windows audio config to see if anything was causing this strange behavior. I've let go of the thought after many tweaks with no positive (or negative) changes.

Have you or anybody else gone through this before?

---

EDIT by @rom1v: Run `scrcpy --audio-output-buffer=10`.
EDIT 2: the default value is 10ms since #6775.

title: [Android 13] V4L2 Sink is empty.
body:
- [Y] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [Y] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: [Debian 11]
 - scrcpy version: [2.0]
 - installation method: [manual build]
 - device model: Samsung A51
 - Android version: [13]

**Describe the bug**

Using the --v42l-sink argument results in an empty, unplayable stream, as showcased by ffplay's output:

```
scrcpy -m 800 --v4l2-sink=/dev/video3 -N

scrcpy 2.0 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
INFO: Video orientation is locked for v4l2 sink. See --lock-video-orientation.
INFO: No display and no recording: audio disabled
/usr/local/share/scrcpy/scrcpy-server: 1 file pushed. 1.5 MB/s (52867 bytes in 0.033s)
[server] INFO: Device: samsung SM-A515F (Android 13)
INFO: v4l2 sink started to device: /dev/video3
```


```
ffplay -i /dev/video3

ffplay version 4.3.5-0+deb11u1 Copyright (c) 2003-2022 the FFmpeg developers
  built with gcc 10 (Debian 10.2.1-6)

...

nan    :  0.000 fd=   0 aq=    0KB vq=    0KB sq=    0B f=0/0
```

The ffmpeg version is the one from Debian's repositories. Using v4l2 without scrcpy works as expected:

```
ffmpeg -i Video.mp4 -f v4l2 /dev/video3

Input #0, mov,mp4,m4a,3gp,3g2,mj2, from 'Video.mp4':
  Metadata:
    major_brand     : isom
    minor_version   : 512
    compatible_brands: isomiso2mp41
    encoder         : Lavf58.29.100
  Duration: 00:00:51.13, start: 0.000000, bitrate: 889 kb/s
    Stream #0:0(und): Video: av1 (Main) (av01 / 0x31307661), yuv420p(tv, bt709), 952x720, 753 kb/s, 60.02 fps, 60 tbr, 15360 tbn, 15360 tbc (default)
    Metadata:
      handler_name    : ISO Media file produced by Google Inc.
    Stream #0:1(und): Audio: aac (LC) (mp4a / 0x6134706D), 44100 Hz, stereo, fltp, 127 kb/s (default)
    Metadata:
      handler_name    : ISO Media file produced by Google Inc.
[libdav1d @ 0x5599ff9be4c0] libdav1d 0.7.1
Stream mapping:
  Stream #0:0 -> #0:0 (av1 (libdav1d) -> rawvideo (native))
Press [q] to stop, [?] for help
Output #0, video4linux2,v4l2, to '/dev/video3':
  Metadata:
    major_brand     : isom
    minor_version   : 512
    compatible_brands: isomiso2mp41
    encoder         : Lavf58.45.100
    Stream #0:0(und): Video: rawvideo (I420 / 0x30323449), yuv420p, 952x720 [SAR 1:1 DAR 119:90], q=2-31, 493516 kb/s, 60 fps, 60 tbn, 60 tbc (default)
    Metadata:
      handler_name    : ISO Media file produced by Google Inc.
      encoder         : Lavc58.91.100 rawvideo
frame=  253 fps=0.0 q=-0.0 Lsize=N/A time=00:00:04.21 bitrate=N/A speed= 4.9x
```


```
ffplay -i /dev/video3

ffplay version 4.3.5-0+deb11u1 Copyright (c) 2003-2022 the FFmpeg developers
  built with gcc 10 (Debian 10.2.1-6)
  configuration: --prefix=/usr --extra-version=0+deb11u1 --toolchain=hardened --libdir=/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu --incdir=/usr/include/x86_64-linux-gnu --arch=amd64 --enable-gpl --disable-stripping --enable-avresample --disable-filter=resample --enable-gnutls --enable-ladspa --enable-libaom --enable-libass --enable-libbluray --enable-libbs2b --enable-libcaca --enable-libcdio --enable-libcodec2 --enable-libdav1d --enable-libflite --enable-libfontconfig --enable-libfreetype --enable-libfribidi --enable-libgme --enable-libgsm --enable-libjack --enable-libmp3lame --enable-libmysofa --enable-libopenjpeg --enable-libopenmpt --enable-libopus --enable-libpulse --enable-librabbitmq --enable-librsvg --enable-librubberband --enable-libshine --enable-libsnappy --enable-libsoxr --enable-libspeex --enable-libsrt --enable-libssh --enable-libtheora --enable-libtwolame --enable-libvidstab --enable-libvorbis --enable-libvpx --enable-libwavpack --enable-libwebp --enable-libx265 --enable-libxml2 --enable-libxvid --enable-libzmq --enable-libzvbi --enable-lv2 --enable-omx --enable-openal --enable-opencl --enable-opengl --enable-sdl2 --enable-pocketsphinx --enable-libmfx --enable-libdc1394 --enable-libdrm --enable-libiec61883 --enable-chromaprint --enable-frei0r --enable-libx264 --enable-shared
  libavutil      56. 51.100 / 56. 51.100

...
```

It's also worth noting that using the H265 codec with any encoder results in an immediate V4L2 error (results above were done with H264):

```
scrcpy --video-codec=h265 -m 800 --v4l2-sink=/dev/video3 -N

scrcpy 2.0 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
INFO: Video orientation is locked for v4l2 sink. See --lock-video-orientation.

INFO: v4l2 sink started to device: /dev/video3
ERROR: [FFmpeg] Unknown V4L2 pixel format equivalent for yuv420p
ERROR: Failed to write header to /dev/video3
WARN: Could not send packet to v4l2 sink
ERROR: Could not send frame to v4l2 sink
^C
```

It might be more appropriate to open a separate issue for this, as I haven't tested this thoroughly.
author: ako294

title: Not work audio streaming on android 11
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows 11
 - scrcpy version: scrcpy 2.0
 - installation method: Windows release...
 - device model: Redmi Note 8T
 - Android version: 11

**Describe the bug**
The sound just doesn't work. When you try to make it forcibly this happens

```
PS Programs\scrcpy-win64-v2.0> .\scrcpy-console.bat -d --require-audio
scrcpy 2.0 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
C:\Users\bropi\Programs\scrcpy-win64-v2.0\scrcpy-server: 1 file pushed, 0 skipped. 62.6 MB/s (52867 bytes in 0.001s)
[server] INFO: Device: Xiaomi Redmi Note 8T (Android 11)
INFO: Renderer: direct3d
INFO: Initial texture: 1080x2336
[server] ERROR: Failed to start audio capture
[server] ERROR: On Android 11, it is only possible to capture in foreground, make sure that the device is unlocked when starting scrcpy.
WARN: Demuxer 'audio': stream explicitly disabled by the device

author: hellishvictor

title: Add H264/H265/AV1 GPU accelerated video decode support
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Currently, scrcpy doesen't use the GPU's hardware accelerated video decoding capabilities, which increases resource usage/decode delay, this doesen't impact most modern PCs much, but on systems with weaker processors (especially with AV1), it should make a big difference, as far as I know, there is no downside to using video decoding acceleration, and most applications already use it as expected (web browsers, telegram/whatsapp, video editing software, every media player in existance... scrcpy is the only application I've ever found that doesen't take advantage of this).   


**Describe the solution you'd like**
Hardware accelerated video decode should be either an option, or enabled by default and togglable with a parameter.   

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

**Additional context**
![H264 scrcpy](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/77066322/224558842-d505a786-22fc-4d73-be95-883a1cf9ccb0.png)
![H264 movie](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/77066322/224558646-8a52e5df-96cf-4620-8725-9bf47acddc90.png)
![H265 scrcpy](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/77066322/224558651-51992ece-b405-4607-bfa0-dfee18dbf1e1.png)
![H265 movie](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/77066322/224558932-eff6ab1f-96a4-43a2-a1fc-61a7e4d20ef7.png)

I observed the lack of gpu video decoding capabilities on a Windows 11 system with an AMD RX 580 GPU running scrcpy v2.0, I'm not sure if hardware decoding is limited to Linux or Nvidia/Intel GPUs, I couldn't find anything regarding this subject in the documentation, so I think it's missing on every system.
I couldn't test the presence of AV1 video decoding capabilities as I don't have an Android device with an AV1 encoder and I don't have a GPU with an AV1 decoder.

While [trying to upgrade](https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/pull/220783), the v2.0 release fails to build on macOS with clang 11 due to errors from the `bool` type template not expanding correctly (as I can gather, my C knowledge is basically non-existent).


```
../app/src/screen.c:821:13: error: expected expression
            bool ok = sc_screen_init_size(screen);
            ^
/nix/store/dkgjv2srx116b0f9qnp9vcy3jjk5f39q-clang-wrapper-11.1.0/resource-root/include/stdbool.h:15:14: note: expanded from macro 'bool'
#define bool _Bool
             ^
../app/src/screen.c:822:18: error: use of undeclared identifier 'ok'
            if (!ok) {
                 ^
1 warning and 2 errors generated.
ninja: build stopped: subcommand failed.
```

At the moment I plan to mark the package as broken for macOS and only support Linux, but it'd be great to have this fixed.

title: ERROR: Could not open audio device: WASAPI can't initialize audio client: CoInitialize has not been called on Windows 7
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows 7 x86/x64
 - scrcpy version: 2.0
 - installation method:  Windows release
 - device model: Model independent error. 
 - Android version: 12

After starting scrcpy-console on Windows 7 x86 or Windows 7 x64 the following error is written to console and the client is closed:
ERROR: Could not open audio device: WASAPI can't initialize audio client: CoInitialize has not been called.
ERROR: Demuxer error
WARN: Killing the server...

It seems to be an SDL2 error. The workaround to use other SDL2 audio driver like winmm or directsound does not work with srccpy since regardless of the buffer size you only get static/garbled audio.

author: xdesniy

title: App lock integration
body:
-  I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
So I have app locks on multiple apps, and so I have to scan my finger or face every time I want to open an app and for me, it got really annoying after like a week or so. Even when I am connected wirelessly, I cannot just take my laptop and move around, I have to take the phone with me everywhere too. Just removing the locks doesn't feel feasible either.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I was hoping that those security inputs could be sent via the laptop directly. Now fingerprint scanner ain't available too widely, but laptops do have the inbuilt camera. I was hoping something like a stream from the camera directly goes as an input to the applock service. Sort of like a virtual camera. Seeing the tons of limitations on android though, this approach seems undoable to me, but I haven't worked with android too much so I figured, you all can come up with some wonderful alternative.
Another thing could be, your own scrcpy applock app for android, which could authenticate the face/finger on the laptop, and send over an unlock ping. For that, you could use something like face-api equivalent on android, but it does kinda feel like going against the spirit of scrcpy working seamlessly without installing any apps on the phone.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I tried sticking the phone on a stand on my desk. That works as it scans the face automatically, but that means, either I don't leave the desk, or I take the phone with me. Tried sticking the stand to the laptop, but that's wobbly and looks funny and I fear the phone can fall over anytime.

**Additional context**
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

body:

**Environment**
 - OS: W10 64-bit
 - scrcpy version: 2.0 64-bit
 - installation method: straight from the zip file 
 - device model: Pixel 4A
 - Android version: 13 (TQ1A.230205.002)

I had received water damage on my Pixel 4A which prevented me from using the screen but I was still able to use it perfectly fine with scrcpy (only wired). I took it in to a shop for an exact diagnosis of whatever damages could have occurred and they disconnected the screen from the motherboard but reassembled my device afterwards (so there is no image output). Now when I connect to scrcpy my framerate is excruciatingly slow, but still technically usable.

How could disconnecting the LCD panel affect framerate through the renderer? Is there a fix for this?

title: OMX.hisi.video.encoder can not mirror video when the screen automatically displayed off
body:
-  I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 -  I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows
 - scrcpy version: 2.0
 - installation method: Windows release
 - device model: HUAWEI COL-AL10
 - Android version: 9

**Describe the bug**


```
.\scrcpy.exe --tcpip=192.168.0.1 --video-encoder=OMX.hisi.video.encoder.hevc -m 1024 --max-fps=25 --power-off-on-close

[server] INFO: Device: HUAWEI COL-AL10 (Android 9)

[server] WARN: Audio disabled: it is not supported before Android 11

INFO: Renderer: direct3d

WARN: Demuxer 'audio': stream explicitly disabled by the device

INFO: Initial texture: 488x1024

WARN: Killing the server...
```

The expectation is, the video mirror equals to what have displayed on the screen of phone. However, my phone has an auto display off in ~ 3 minutes if no touching, or, if I use MOD+M or MOD+P to awake the screen but does not unlock the phone, the screen will be off in ~10 seconds. (It is trivial I guess)

In the case, MOD+M or MOD+P can awake the physical screen and mouse can toggle the screen, but the mirror keeps black. No terminal info provided.

If I manually MOD+P to screen off then MOD+P again instantly, it works. Only when the screen set off by Android itself, the sync breaks.

It is not 100%, but ~70% in daily use.

It has 3 encoders:

```
[server] INFO: List of video encoders:
    --video-codec=h264 --video-encoder='OMX.hisi.video.encoder.avc'
    --video-codec=h264 --video-encoder='OMX.google.h264.encoder'
    --video-codec=h265 --video-encoder='OMX.hisi.video.encoder.hevc'
```

Only in OMX.hisi.video.encoder.avc or OMX.hisi.video.encoder.hevc, it happens.

title: Video encoding
body:
- [✓ ] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The current video encoding codecs are not fast enough, since they are more optimized for quality and size rather than speed. This compromises latency and causes video to be choppy and laggy. Ruins a use for scrcpy (gaming).

**Describe the solution you'd like**
More encoding codecs to be implemented such as vp8, vp9, h263, avc, mpeg etc. Thus, the best one can be chosen depending on the user's use case. In my case, low latency, but doesn't care about quality too much.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Transmitting the video raw but it is not viable because the transfer speeds won't be able to keep up.

**Additional context**
Most of the smartphones have hardware encoders for the codecs mentioned above. It is only natural that scrcpy adds support for them.

body:
- [ √ ] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows
 - scrcpy version: 2.0
 - installation method: Windows release
 - device model: Redmi K50 Pro
 - Android version: 13

**Describe the bug**

Extremely laggy while device is connected wirelessly.Stdout reported many frames skipped.No errors.

Here are the videos.
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/105844676/227729878-c7e9c780-2a44-41c8-845c-6b622313c6f4.mp4
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/105844676/227729884-040d6b03-5837-47bd-a9f1-a9904166dc57.mp4



```
INFO: 7 fps (+7 frames skipped)
INFO: 9 fps (+4 frames skipped)
INFO: 0 fps
INFO: 7 fps (+15 frames skipped)
INFO: 6 fps
INFO: 9 fps (+7 frames skipped)
INFO: 9 fps
INFO: 6 fps (+7 frames skipped)
INFO: 7 fps (+3 frames skipped)
```

Tried lowering the resolution,bitrates and fps by adding "-m1024 -b2M --max-fps=30", which didn't help.

I'm using Wi-Fi 6 router and Redmi K50 Pro reports its Wi-Fi speed as 2400Mbps. 
While it's connected wirelessly, I noticed that my download/upload speed indicator on status bar didn't even reach above 1M/s, which is strange beacause transmitting video data should cost lots of bandwidth.

While it's connected via usb, its smooth and there isn't frames skipped.

title: [scrcpy 2.0] v4l2 output is broken ("[h264 @ 0xHex] No start code is found")
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Arch Linux
 - scrcpy version: 2.0
 - installation method: Arch `community` repository
 - device model: OnePlus 7 Pro
 - Android version: 10

**Describe the bug**
Small bit of context: I use scrcpy primarily as a way to use my device as a webcam in OBS.

In scrcpy 1.25, the v4l2 output works as expected in OBS, as well as other apps that use webcam on Linux through v4l2 (i.e. Discord)
In scrcpy 2.0, which the `community` repo just pushed as of a day ago, the v4l2 output seems to still be pushed as expected:
```
scrcpy --v4l2-sink=/dev/video0 --v4l2-buffer=300 --video-bit-rate=30M --max-fps=60 --lock-video-orientation=3 --crop=1440:2560:0:364 --video-codec=h264 -N -S -w --power-off-on-close
Detected device 64943115
scrcpy 2.0 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
INFO: No display and no recording: audio disabled
/usr/share/scrcpy/scrcpy-server: 1 file pushed, 0 skipped. 695.8 MB/s (52867 bytes in 0.000s)
[server] INFO: Device: OnePlus OnePlus 7 Pro (Android 10)
INFO: v4l2 sink started to device: /dev/video0
[server] INFO: Device screen turned off
```
...however, when I open OBS, the video capture element reserved for the scrcpy v4l2-loopback device is black. I notice that the video format dropdown has changed, from "Planar YUV 4:2:0" to "YU12 (emulated)" and there's a new format labeled "H.264". But selecting this doesn't change anything - in fact, the output seems to fail.

*(truncated OBS tty output just to show the relevant symptom):*
```

title: "--display=id" not seen on linux version
body: I think "--display=id"  option isnt available on linux version. So in case of multiple display's connected to a device, I see a possibility currently only in Windows version of scrcpy. It'll be best if we can have this functionality on Linux as well, as most of the Embedded application especially in the Automotive domain, rely on Linux.

title: How to get video stream over USB? Not use tcp
body:
Hello everyone,
I want to get video stream and use in electron app. 
Now I use tcp:1234 for getting video stream data from device. 
```
adb push scrcpy-server-v1.25 /data/local/tmp/scrcpy-server.jar
adb forward tcp:1234 localabstract:scrcpy
adb shell CLASSPATH=/data/local/tmp/scrcpy-server.jar \
    app_process / com.genymobile.scrcpy.Server 1.25 \
    tunnel_forward=true control=false cleanup=false \
    max_size=1600 raw_video_stream=true
```
But i get slow when display on app. When I use Scrcpy, it displays good because it connect and stream data via USB. 
Question: 
1. How can i get video stream via USB without TCP port. ?
2. When use tcp, i have to build a Node server to listen data from TCP:1234 and emit to app. 
How can i get video stream data with current Adb server in pc? 
Thank all you.

title: Notification audio not being forwarded (e.g. Phone call or message)
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: [Windows 11 22H2]
 - scrcpy version: [2.0]
 - Installation method: [Windows Release]
 - Device model: Pixel 6
 - Android version: 13

**Describe the bug**
Whenever a call is received, the ringtone is played through the phone's speakers and not the windows playback device.

**Questions**
Is this intended behavior?
Is the API capable of handling such events?

**Possible Solution**
If the API is incapable, I would not be opposed to having an optional notification. The notification could just show up natively on windows stating "You have received a notification" and then clicking it would take you to strcpy (possibly even open the notification window).

**Final Note**
Thank you for creating this, it is an amazing tool!

title: Brew unable to install/ upgrade to scrcpy 2.0 on MacOS M1/ M2
body:
- [X] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [X] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: MacOS M1/ M2
 - scrcpy version: 2.0
 - installation method: brew

**Describe the bug**
Today, someone told me they could not install scrcpy using Brew on their M1 Macbook. The build errors out when upgrading glibc to 2.76.1 on MacOS M1.

I also repro-ed by trying to upgrade `brew upgrade scrcpy` on my own M1 Macbook. See the brew build logs below

```

title: [Feature Request] Run commands/script on disconnect
body:
- [X] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
A momentary network disruption would break the running `scrcpy` connection, as expected. However, this leaves the phone in the  unlocked state it was in when `scrcpy` was being used, posing a <ins>potential security and/or privacy risk</ins>. Also since the introduction of the new audio forwarding feature, if loud media was being played on the phone but listened through headphones on PC, the <ins>media continues to play on the phone loudspeaker in these situations, which may be undesirable</ins>. Finally, I run `adb shell wm size` to make the screen size more comfortable for desktop use before starting the mirroring session, which is reverted when `scrcpy` window is closed via my launcher script. A network problem could prevent the final `adb shell wm resize` revert action from being run on the device which <ins>leaves it with a display setting unsuitable for phone use</ins>.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Option to specify a shell script placed locally on the device to be run by `scrcpy-server` on exit (whether normal or abnormal). Alternatively, in a similar fashion to how the `scrcpy-server` binary is uploaded, the script could be kept on the PC and uploaded on each connect. In this script, the user would be able to run any cleanup tasks specific to their setup. This would certainly at least solve the problems I stated above, by allowing me to run `input keyevent 26` (lock screen), `input keyevent VOLUME_MUTE`, and `wm resize` locally in the phone on disconnect.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I wonder if as a workaround, it might be possible to somehow detect the `scrcpy` disconnect on the phone, but that seems like a more fragile solution than the one proposed. Not much else, but any input on alternatives/workarounds would be highly appreciated.

**Additional context**
N/A.

author: yassine-cc

title: Device Overheating issue
body:
When I am mirroring my phone and playing games like Call of Duty: Mobile or Apex Legends, in the first few minutes, the video latency is noticeable, but it is less than a second, so not that much of an issue. But after playing for 5–10 minutes, my phone overheats, and it becomes totally unusable for a while. The video latency also increases to about 10 seconds.

But after playing some lightweight games like Alto's Adventure for a while, the video latency increases to about 3–4 seconds. The phone heats up a bit but does not overheat like it did before. My laptop does not heat up at all while using the software.

What might be the issue? Is it the hardware limitation of my device, or is it the software itself? If it is my hardware limitation, then what is the required specification for my PC and my phone to run the application smoothly?

title: Suggest adding detection and authorization for 'abd root' to ensure successful Scrcpy
body:
Some Chinese mobile phone manufacturers may modify the Android system, resulting in "abd root": add cannot run as root in production builds.

At this point, Scrcpy cannot transfer Scrcpy server to the phone's/data/local/tmp; Scrcpy failed to run！
>adb:error:failed to copy 'D:\Tools\Scr cpy\scr cpy-server'to'/data/local/tmp/scr cpy-server.jar':remote secure_mkdirs failed:Permission denied
ERROR:"adb push" returned with value 1
ERROR:Server connection failed

By using the following method, 'abd root' can be successful: adbd is already running as root
At this point, Scrcpy runs successfully.
1. Need to create a new *. sh script file(I named this file  ‘start_adb_root.sh’ );
>#!/system/bin/sh
resetprop ro.adb.secure 0
resetprop ro.secure 0
/data/adb/magisk/busybox pkill -9 adbd
su -c adbd

2. The PC needs to execute the following commands:
>FOR /F "delims=" %%b IN ('forfiles /S /M "start_adb_root.sh" /C "cmd /c echo @path"') DO set start_adb_root=%%b
adb shell magisk su -c sh<%start_adb_root%
timeout /nobreak /t 3 >nul 2>nul

[server] WARN: Display doesn't have FLAG_SUPPORTS_PROTECTED_BUFFERS flag, mirroring can be restricted
Which I didn't get before.

Used to let me use dex and keep the phone off.

I am using a USB c dock with an HDMI display terminator-I believe that is still necessary.

title: Installing in Windows with winget does not update the path properly
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows 10 22H2 19045.2846
 - scrcpy version: 1.25
 - installation method: winget
 - device model: MacBook Pro with BootCamp
 - Android version: not applicable

**Describe the bug**

1. Open Windows Terminal with PowerShell 7.3.4
2. Run `winget install Genymobile.scrcpy`
3. Restart Windows Terminal
4. Run `scrcpy`

- Expected: scrcpy starts

- Actual: Error message is shown in red: _scrcpy : The term 'scrcpy ' is not recognized as a name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or executable program. Check the spelling of the name, or if a path was included, verify that the path is correct and try again._

Checking the PATH environment variable reveals that the path configured is:
`C:\Users\{MI-USER}\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WinGet\Packages\Genymobile.scrcpy_Microsoft.Winget.Source_8wekyb3d8bbwe`

This folder contains another folder named `scrcpy-win64-v1.25`, which contains the binaries.

**Temporary fix**
Update the PATH environment variable to include `\scrcpy-win64-v1.25`.

body:
- [V ] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [V ] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: [e.g. Debian, Windows, macOS...]
 Windows

 - scrcpy version: [e.g. 1.12.1]
 2.0

 - installation method: [e.g. manual build, apt, snap, brew, Windows release...]
Extract from the ZIP file

 - device model:
Pixel 6

 - Android version: [e.g. 10]
 13

**Describe the bug**
I've launched an app that decided to change the device orientation after some time. During this time, I was actually using a very different window on Windows OS.
When it changed the orientation, ScrCpy stole focus. As it was in a full screen online game, it was very annoying because I lost the context and had to quickly switch back.
When I tried to reproduce it via a sample, I've also noticed that if ScrCpy is just minimized to the taskbar, it will close itself completely when the orientation changes.
The command I use for ScrCpy is:

```
C:\android\scrcpy\scrcpy-noconsole.vbs -S --always-on-top --stay-awake --no-audio
```

Here's a sample app to simulate this, which changes the orientation after 5 seconds since shown. During this time, switch to another window, and wait for it to happen.

[My Application.zip](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/files/11314795/My.Application.zip)
[app-debug.zip](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/files/11314796/app-debug.zip)


Code:


```
class MainActivity : AppCompatActivity() {
    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
        setContentView(R.layout.activity_main)
        Handler(mainLooper).postDelayed({
            requestedOrientation = if (resources.configuration.orientation == ActivityInfo.SCREEN_ORIENTATION_PORTRAIT)
                ActivityInfo.SCREEN_ORIENTATION_LANDSCAPE
            else
                ActivityInfo.SCREEN_ORIENTATION_PORTRAIT
        },  5000L)
    }
}
```


On errors, please provide the output of the console (and `adb logcat` if relevant).
There are no errors.

However, here's a video showing the issue:
[2023-04-25_00-34-32.zip](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/files/11314854/2023-04-25_00-34-32.zip)

And here's a video showing the issue when there is another window, on top, that needs to stay on focus (a video player app) :

[2023-04-25_00-42-13_1.zip](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/files/11314895/2023-04-25_00-42-13_1.zip)

title: Touch mutually exclusive, the computer control interrupts the phone touch screen operation.
body:
**Environment**
 - OS: macOS
 - scrcpy version: 2.0
 - installation method: brew
 - device model: Xiaomi 10
 - Android version: 13

I read and write English relying on translations, so I'm not sure if I'm missing documentation or already existing issues, so please don't blame me haha.

I found a touch-related problem when using scrcpy: I look for any interface with a drop-down refresh on my phone and keep the drop-down state un-relaxed, then using mouse clicks or swiping the screen on the computer side of scrcpy will break my phone side touch causing the drop-down to unwind, is this a potential problem?
I have observed: some gamepads that rely on adb permission touch mapping do not have such problems.
Is there a magic that makes the touchscreen and other analog touches work at the same time?

body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I use the "--turn-screen-off" option, but sometimes an event (like an incoming call for example) turns it back on.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
add a `--keep-screen-off` option

make the `--turn-screen-off` option keep the screen always off.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I use the *Turn device screen off* `MOD+o` shortcut on incoming call.

title: Why not use the copy and paste function to solve text input problems in windows?
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

It seems that physical keyboard simulation does not work in windows with mirroring. Therefore, it is a disaster when entering large amounts of text. We can only type non-English words by plainly switching between mouse with keyboard. Why not use existing methods to solve non-English text input problems?

**Describe the solution you'd like**

As the documentation says, adb only supports the English alphabet, but the clipboard is unaffected. I have tested it this many times, which can type large sections of non-English text by copying and pasting. I input the text in Notepad first and then copy and paste it into SCRCPY when I'm done typing. I think this way is ok with any text editing software. The only problem is to  switching between Notepad and SCRCPY. However, this is still a relatively efficient method of text input.

so, why not add a input box and a "send" button at the edge of the SCRCPY window. we can type text in the box and click the button (or shortcut keys) to cut the text in box then paste it to SCRCPY.

title: Why re-set a new encoder after encode one frame?
body: (https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/cb20bcb16f4ca191e237c8744a7c2e6d29701d60/server/src/main/java/com/genymobile/scrcpy/ScreenEncoder.java#L97)
author: wnpllrzodiac

title: Scrcpy lagging while streaming to OBS Studio
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows
 - scrcpy version: 2.0
 - installation method: Windows release
 - device model: Redmi Note 10 Pro (Indian Variant)
 - Android version: 13

**Describe the bug**
I tried to stream my phone screen with SCRCPY using OBS Studio. I want it as a web cam so I open my Camera app (Open Camera) and streamed it via scrcpy. But after sometime it starts lagging. I think the right term for it would be "frame skipping", I don't really know. I have a pretty strong laptop specs and its not even consumed fully while streaming.

The settings I use in SCRCPY:
scrcpy -m 1280 -b 8M --max-fps=30 --turn-screen-off --stay-awake

The lag doesn't seem to be on my phone or Laptops ends. Its either in OBS or Scrcpy. I also came across this another term "Encoder Overloading" which I felt like could be the culprit but I am using my GPU as my encoder.

My Laptop Specs: Lenovo Legion I5 10th Gen NVIDIA 1650 16GB RAM 1.5 GB SSD

Please help with a solution on what to do to avoid this issue.
author: i-am-vimal

I don't know if this is a bug or not, but my phone is getting hot every time I watch a video on the device using scrcpy in the fullscreen mode. usually, my phone gets hot after 30 minutes of being plugged into the computer (scrcpy). author: IMMORTALZANTA

title: No video (black screen) with Now TV Android app v0.36.0
body:
Hey guys, first off let me thank you very, very much for a great software package I have been using for more than three years! My way of using `scrcpy` was (unfortunately in the past tense now) to connect my Android phone to my Linux laptop, and have the Formula 1 race, that I'd watch using the Now TV app, on a nice big external screen. Now TV (the only official way to stream Formula 1 in the UK) have a ridiculous approach not to support streaming over the browser, and they make you use their silly player (which is very brittle, and has zero support for Linux); the only alternative is to use the mobile Android app (which used to work very well with `scrcpy` before `nowtv=36.0`) and get the video on a big external monitor. Unfortunately this has stopped working with `scrcpy`, and it's clearly a setting in their new version of the app, nothing to do with your software, but am wondering if you could give me a few pointers, if it can be fixed, please. See below for problem description:

**Problem summary**

(live) video stream from Android app Now TV appears black (is not shown) on the laptop hooked to the phone with `scrcpy`. Everything else in-app (Now TV app) and off phone appears fine on the laptop, the only thing that is not transferred over is the actual video. See screenshots (first showing all is fine when connecting phone to laptop, second showing the actual stream window, missing the video)

![gen1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/28983971/236815761-7c76e53e-3c39-4b6f-9e8c-95d6db6d8863.png)
![gen2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/28983971/236815787-c71a0975-2138-4f0b-a2c2-589347f3ea1b.png)

**Versions tried and debug info**

Problem first appeared when Now TV released their latest version of the app 36.0, before that `scrcpy=1.25` and `nowtv=35.0` and earlier worked very well. I also tested with `scrcpy=2.0` and it's the same problem, also tested with two different Android devices (phones), see below for debug output:

```
on Moto G4 (that has worked like a charm until nowtv=0.36.0)

and

I frankly can't see anything from the debug logs, all seems to be interpreted correctly by `scrcpy`. Very many thanks in advance, and I hope you can help me a bit here; also, please let me know if you need more debugging from me (it'll have to be with `scrcpy=2.0` since I've nuked the old 1.25 installation now). Cheers :beer:

title: How did your program achieve such fast response time?
body:
Hello,

I noticed that when using your Scrcpy program, the interaction with the Android device through ADB is very fast, without any noticeable delays. I'm using the AdvancedSharpAdbClient library in my C# project to interact with an Android device through ADB, but I've noticed a significant delay and not as much responsiveness as in Scrcpy.

I would like to know how your program achieved such a fast response time and responsiveness when working with an Android device through ADB. I would be grateful if you could share your knowledge and experience in this area and offer any recommendations or advice for optimizing the ADB interaction in my project using the AdvancedSharpAdbClient library.

**Describe the bug**

So I was fascinated with the new update, specifically the OTG mode because it works perfectly with minecraft, so of course i tried it also on another apps and they seem to be working fine but the problem is:

When I tried it on a game called "Mobile Legends: Bang Bang" (aka MLBB) no mouse inputs are accepted. Scroll, clicks, hovering, none worked, be reminded that before and after encountering this issue I tried to use it on other apps and they were working good so I'm wondering if is this an app-specific bug or what.

Edit 1:

Please paste terminal output in a code block.

 `scrcpy.exe --otg -M -K`

author: elf-world

title: Mouse capture bug when playing minecraft
body:
- [ /] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [ /] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: [android]
 - scrcpy version: [2.0]
 - installation method: [Windows release]
 - device model: infinix hot 20
 - Android version: [12

**Describe the bug**

So I was playing minecraft but whenever i click the save and quit option, the mouse goes back to mouse captured state even after doing alt+tab then clicking back on that android icon. It's either i directly quit the game and risk it being not saved or i have to restart the whole  scrcpy app again, can this be fixed?


Please paste terminal output in a code block.
```scrcpy --otg -M -K```
author: john-basilio

title: failed to connect to socket 'tcp:21784' when using VPN to communicate
body:
**-Scenario：**
Device(USB) --> Pc A --> VPN <---> VPN --> Server --> scrcpy

What I'm trying to do is to let the Server use scrcpy to control the device which has connected to Pc A through VPN 
(PC A and Server both are using vpn)

**Step/ Configuration I've done:**

**Pc A**
```
- adb kill-server
- adb -a -P 5037 nodaemon server start

```
**Server**
```
- adb kill-server
- ssh -CN -L5037:localhost:5037 -L27184:localhost:27184 user@Pc A's VPN IP address
- **Another Terminal**
-scrcpy -p 27184 --force-adb-forward -Vdebug
```

**-Output from server scrcpy**
```
scrcpy 2.0 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
DEBUG: ADB device found:
DEBUG:     -->   (usb)           R38MC037W7X            device  SM_G970U1

DEBUG: Remaining connection attempts: 100
