[server] INFO: Device: samsung SM-G970U1 (Android 12)
DEBUG: Remaining connection attempts: 98
...
DEBUG: Remaining connection attempts: 1
DEBUG: Server disconnected
DEBUG: Server terminated
ERROR: Server connection failed
```

-Output from PC A terminal
```
adb E 05-12 15:19:23 27260 26172 services.cpp:83] failed to connect to socket 'tcp:21784': cannot connect to 127.0.0.1:21784: No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it. (10061)
adb E 05-12 15:20:07 27260 26172 services.cpp:83] failed to connect to socket 'tcp:21784': cannot connect to 127.0.0.1:21784: No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it. (10061)
adb E 05-12 15:20:09 27260 26172 services.cpp:83] failed to connect to socket 'tcp:21784': cannot connect to 127.0.0.1:21784: No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it. (10061)
```
And another way I tried without ssh tunnel is:
```
>scrcpy --tunnel-host=172.xx.xx.xx --no-audio -Vdebug
scrcpy 2.0 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
INFO: Tunnel host/port is set, --force-adb-forward automatically enabled.
DEBUG: ADB device found:
DEBUG:     -->   (usb)           R38MC037W7X            device  SM_G970U1
DEBUG: Device serial: R38MC037W7X

BUG: Interrupting process
ERROR: "adb forward --remove" returned with value 1

```

Question:
Is there any configuration that I missing? or need to forward PC A's VPN port to PC A's local port?
author: yiyang1016

title: How to parse Server's "frame meta"?
body:
Hi, I'm trying to write a go implementation of the Scrcpy client. When I insert the obtained h264 stream data into the libavcodec's decoder, I sometimes get this error
```
[h264 @ 0x2e56a40] No start code is found.
[h264 @ 0x2e56a40] Error splitting the input into NAL units.
4 Invalid data found when processing input
```
I think this is because I am not splitting the NALU data correctly and wrapping it into the AVPacket.
To simplify the code, as you did in #646, I want to parse the `frame meta` data to read the complete NALU packet one by one.
I didn't understand the Server java code and I'm using version 1.25.
Can you give me some clues?
author: nnnpa31

title: Nintendo hinders the development of scrcpy
body:
![totk](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/assets/543275/902e3588-0583-40e0-ac9c-6bb120bc84be)

title: Is there is a native way to automate selection of USB/tcpip conection?
body:
### **Problem:** You can't have an automated selection of best possible connection with the phone.

So there is -select-usb and --select-tcpip, and if your phone is conected thrue USB and tcpip, you forsed to use one of them or --serial.

In `device.md` i did not found a native way to "if USB is connected then select USB and if it does not then select tcpip".

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Command that alows to automate devide selection for example "--select-usb-tcpip".

**Behavior:**
- If there is only 2 devices: one usb and another tcpip then usb is selected.
- If only one then: that one is selected.

 - device model: Pixel 7, 
 - Android version: Android 14 Beta (UpsideDownCake)

**Describe the bug**
Android 14 beta lock orientation doesn't work as expected when the device goes into landscape mode.
eg: if you launch: `scrcpy --lock-video-orientation=0` then go into landscape in an app, you get this:
![image](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/assets/1129490/302b3db9-0094-443a-bf88-7c2a6264d0da)

Looks like it no longer rotates the display output to fit the surface of the codec, but instead it keeps it in its orientation and scales it. Not clear if its a "bug" which will be fixed in the release, but seems possibly unlikely since its already in beta.

title: Faulty symlink when using WinGet
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows 10
 - scrcpy version: 2.0
 - installation method: winget
 - device model: Lenovo P1M
 - Android version: 5.1

**Describe the bug**
After installing through winget, a symlink is generated at `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\WinGet\Links` as a mean of getting the executable into Path. However, when executing the symlink through the terminal (PowerShell 7.3.4 Core on Win10), it doesn't execute anything, just outputting nothing and returning to prompt. Even though manually going through the directory using File Explorer and opening it by double-clicking will launch it as normal.

```ps
 ASUS  PS  Monday 09:20:08 PM
┌ C:\Users\ASUS
└ ❯ scrcpy

 ASUS  PS  Monday 09:20:12 PM
┌ C:\Users\ASUS
└ ❯
```

And no, the custom PS profile isn't interfering with anything. Heck, running with `-noprofile` will return an error instead, as if there was no symlink at all.

```ps
PowerShell 7.3.4
PS C:\Users\ASUS> scrcpy
scrcpy: The term 'scrcpy' is not recognized as a name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or executable program.
Check the spelling of the name, or if a path was included, verify that the path is correct and try again.
PS C:\Users\ASUS>
```

However, executing the command via cmd.exe results in a stream of error prompts instead, reading: "The code execution cannot proceed because [multiple DLLs] was not found. Reinstalling the program may fix this problem."
author: headquarter8302

title: Regarding the necessity of killing the adb service in the otg mode on Windows platform
body:
- [ yes ] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Regarding the necessity of killing the adb service in the otg mode on Windows platform**
We can not use HID and mirror mode on Windows platform beacuse Windows not possible to open a USB device by more than one process, and mirror mode will invoke adb server to connect device.  Adb will open our Android USB device.

But really ?

In fact, I managed to use the HID at the same time with the mirroring screen turned on successfully, on Windows platform, used scrcpy.

The key to the way is to use the remote debugging feature of adb, so that adb does not need to use USB to connect the device

First, we open a scrcpy

```script.exe --otg -K```

I will kill adb server and conncet to device, simulate HID input.

Then, we open another scrcpy, use TCP/IP  to connect the device

```script.exe --tcpip=192.168.12.34:5678```

If all goes well, you can now have HID input while mirroring is enabled

![20230523202813](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/assets/46065109/256163d1-d3b2-4316-bf36-4aedb431d3dd)

**My soluction: try connect to device by usb first?**
My mind is, on OTG mode, try to make a connection to the device first instead of killing adb first, If the connection fails, it is not too late to try to kill adb.

On mirror mode, if user use tcpip to connect to device, maybe we can allow simultaneous HID input from the user.


**I am proud to contribute code**
If you think what I'm saying makes sense, I'd be happy to contribute code to complete this functionality. <(￣︶￣)↗[GO!]

title: Add an option to keep the adb daemon alive in OTG mode
body:
- [x] ~~I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.~~

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
According to the [doc](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/2d3059e1ab687a18123636784f1e98ac653d3469/doc/hid-otg.md#physical-keyboard-simulation), users cannot use keyboard simulation with mirroring over the same USB connection on Windows. To monitor the phone and control it at the same time, I need to use adb over TCP and keyboard simulation in OTG mode.
However, If I start scrcpy for monitoring first and start another instance in otg mode for keyboard simulation, the latter will kill the adb daemon. So I have to start the scrcpy in otg mode first, then start scrcpy for monitoring.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
To start the two instances mentioned above in any sequence, I'd like to add an option to prevent killing adb daemon in otg mode([code there](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/2d3059e1ab687a18123636784f1e98ac653d3469/app/src/usb/scrcpy_otg.c#L83-L90)).

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
+ For users like me, start the instances in specified order.
+ Kill the adb daemon only if the target device is connected via USB.
+ #4028 (I haven't test it yet.)

**Additional context**
I know the code for killing the adb daemon is for accessing the device via otg. However, a running adb daemon doesn't means that the target device is connected via USB. I think adding an extra option is easy to be implemented without changing the current behavior. I'm also ready to implement this if you'd like to.
author: wh201906

title: A better method to simulate HID-Keyboard and HID-Mouse for Android device
body:
https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/master/samples/uhid/uhid-example.c

We can utilize the Linux uhid API to create a HID input device, and having ADB permission is sufficient to achieve this.

This method doesn't require an actual USB wire to be connected to the Android device, and allows the HID keyboard and mouse functionality to coexist with other features of scrcpy.

However, this method requires scrcpy to load an ELF file, as the uhid API must be called in JNI.

author: AZYsGithub

title: Mute/audio forward disable on shortcut
body:
- [ X] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.  (Don't worry, this isn't yet another "disable on PC and reenable on phone while running" request.)

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The audio forwarding feature is a great add -- but I find that even when I am using it, it would be nice to be able to mute/disable it for short periods to do something else audio-related with the host machine.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
It'd be great to have a host-side keyboard shortcut to toggle muting/disabling the audio on the host side.  (It's effectively a server-side mute, but been avoiding using "mute" to not conflate it with muting the phone itself.)

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Muting the phone itself: ...I mean, it works, sorta.  But it's definitely unwieldy -- even the existing volume up/down shortcuts just mirror the phone's vol up/down, meaning sometimes they affect media audio, sometimes call audio, sometimes alerts.  Mute itself on phones is of course the same way -- depending on the phone, you may mute media but still get alerts, or mute calls but still get media audio (this happens _very_ often).  It'd be great to have a host-side "master switch" for audio that can just be toggled off and on.

**Additional context**
None come to mind. Thank you for a fantastically useful tool!

title: Windows 10 - virus
body:
Window 10 just informed me that my .vbs script contained a “severe” virus,
then went ahead and deleted it automatically.
Luckily I had it backed up in a .txt file somewhere else,
but if I try to make it again, Windows 10 immediately sees it, flags it, and deletes it again.
This was the content of the .vbs file:


```
' OnePlus 9
strCommand = "cmd /c scrcpy.exe --window-x 1067 --window-y 79 --window-width 463 --window-height 1028 --prefer-text --no-key-repeat --push-target=/sdcard/ --turn-screen-off"

For Each Arg In WScript.Arguments
    strCommand = strCommand & " """ & replace(Arg, """", """""""""") & """"
Next

CreateObject("Wscript.Shell").Run strCommand, 0, false


' --crop 180:70:0:0              ' width:height:X:Y
' --window-title 'notification'
' --window-x 1018
' --window-y 152
' --window-width 88
' --window-height 32
' --window-borderless
' --always-on-top
' --stay-awake           -w
' --turn-screen-off      -S
' --prefer-text
' --raw-key-events
' --no-key-repeat
' --push-target=/sdcard/
' 
' https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy#window-configuration
```
author: nr1995

title: minimum screen mirroring area
body:
I noticed that there seems to be a minimum number of mirrored pixels
below which SCRCPY won't work / won't open the mirror window.
Is it mentioned somewhere how many pixels that is,
and more importantly, can that number be reduced even further?
What I'm trying to do is only mirror the tiny little area where a “1” will appear
if my chosen IM app gets a new incoming message.

title: macOS: warning when opening video with Quicktime: "This file contains some media which isn’t compatible with QuickTime Player."
body: - [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md). - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues). **Environment** - OS: macOS - scrcpy version: 2.0_1 - installation method: brew - device model: Pixel 7 - Android version: 13 **Describe the bug** I'm not sure what changed or if this is even an issue with scrcpy, but just recently when capturing videos from my Pixel7 I've noticed the following popup on Quicktime when opening them. I've never seen this before using scrcpy and the same mac OS (13.1). I should note that hitting Continue still opens the video and it plays back fine. Any ideas if there's something I can do in scrcpy to prevent this? Again, it might just be a Mac/Quicktime issue but I figured I'd have better luck seeing if anyone else has also seen this too? Here's how I'm launching scrcpy: ``` scrcpy -s $ADB_DEVICE $ADB_PORT --show-touches --stay-awake --window-x 100 --window-y 10 --record $FILENAME -V debug ``` I thought maybe audio recording might be the issue so I disabled it but still got the same result. Here's what Quicktime is reporting for video details

title: Screen projection is not possible with scrcpy-server v2.0
body:
I just use scrcpy-server to get the video stream and display it on the web. After the recent update from v1.24 to v2.0, the screen does not display. Do the corresponding parameters need to be modified? Help me check. The bite_rate in the startup parameter has been modified to video_bit_rate

        commands = [
            "adb",
            "-s",
            self.serialno,
            "shell",
            f"CLASSPATH=/data/local/tmp/{JAR_NAME}.jar",
            "app_process",
            "/",
            "com.genymobile.scrcpy.Server",
            SCRCPY_JAR_VERSION,
            "log_level=info",
            f"max_size={self.max_size}",
            f"max_fps={self.max_fps}",
            f"video_bit_rate={self.bitrate}",
            "tunnel_forward=true",
            "send_frame_meta=false",
            "control=true",

title: Integrate with mediakeys dbus extension (MPRIS)
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

Thanks for scrcpy! I'm always surprised how seamless this tool works!

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
With the introduction of audio forward in scrcpy 2.0, I want to start using my smartphone as my main media player. While sound and mouse/keyboard control works nicely I wished that it could integrate with my Linux Desktop even better.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

It would be awesome if scrcpy could interact with the [MPRIS](https://specifications.freedesktop.org/mpris-spec/latest/) interface to support media buttons and maybe even report the current play state/artist/title from android (I'm not sure if that's possible though)

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

Currently I'm using the spotify webinterface as kind of "remote control" to make the media buttons work on linux. While this is enough for my use case, it is clumsy and only works with spotify.

The keys 1, 2, 3, 3 (upper keys above the first line of characters azerty) are not well mapped. The displayed characters on the Smartphone correspond to the QWERTY keyboard: ! in place of 1, @ in place of 2, # in place of 3, etc.
Some others keys are also not correctly mapped: > in place of "."

author: sky-gg

title: install_release.sh wrong SERVER_URL and SHA256
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Debian bookworm (latest)
 - scrcpy version: 2.0
 - installation method: manual build
 - device model: not relevant
 - Android version: not relevant

**Describe the bug**
After a successful installation, scrcpy won´t run because install_release.sh pulled an old version of the scrcpy-server binary.

On errors, please provide the output of the console (and `adb logcat` if relevant).

```
$ scrcpy --no-audio
scrcpy 2.0 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
/usr/local/share/scrcpy/scrcpy-server: 1 file pushed, 0 skipped. 20.2 MB/s (42151 bytes in 0.002s)
[server] ERROR: Exception on thread Thread[main,5,main]
java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: The server version (1.25) does not match the client (2.0)
	at com.genymobile.scrcpy.Server.createOptions(Server.java:166)
	at com.genymobile.scrcpy.Server.main(Server.java:330)
	at com.android.internal.os.RuntimeInit.nativeFinishInit(Native Method)
	at com.android.internal.os.RuntimeInit.main(RuntimeInit.java:355)
ERROR: Server connection failed
```

The solution was to get the url for the v2.0 binary, calculate the SHA256 hash and replace the values on the script:

```
PREBUILT_SERVER_URL=https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/releases/download/v1.25/scrcpy-server-v1.25
PREBUILT_SERVER_SHA256=ce0306c7bbd06ae72f6d06f7ec0ee33774995a65de71e0a83813ecb67aec9bdb
```

to:

```
PREBUILT_SERVER_URL=https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/releases/download/v2.0/scrcpy-server-v2.0
PREBUILT_SERVER_SHA256=9e241615f578cd690bb43311000debdecf6a9c50a7082b001952f18f6f21ddc2
```

And run the script again. Now scrcpy runs fine.

I suppose those values on the script should be generated automatically using a template or something when generating the release.

Cheers!

it's a new feature, NOT related to a problem. this tools is very powerful and roburst. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
when scrcpy started, i want press a shortcut like ctrl+r do start record screen to a video file, and after a while, if i press another shortcut or same shortcut, recording is stopped and the video file is written.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
nothing add-on. thanks.

**Additional context**
nothing add-on. thanks.

author: rom1v

title: Turn in: Issue's still here. Scrcpy v2.1
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Linux Mint 20.2
 - scrcpy version: 2.1
 - installation method: Manual Build
 - device model: LG K10 M250
 - Android version: 7.0

**Describe the bug**
"Let's plug your phone in using this cable so you can talk to it wirelessly because Scrcpy can't look for a random port, it has to be 5555".
Updated scrcpy to v2.1.
Unfortunately this issue has been going on and is still a thing.
I find it inconvenient having to connect my phone via USB, so I can "enable" ADB to talk to it on port 5555; It defeats the purpose.

I did my research and was able to "get around" this by rooting my device and have it "free" another port, to which SCRCPY uses to connect to it. That works but it's not the end of the MAIN issue because it requires a rooted phone. Meaning I'm no longer able to transfer files by drag-and-drop, nor am I able to open all of my banking apps -- I literally have to restart my device so it becomes unrooted again -- so it's inconvenient because either I'm using Scrcpy or my essential apps.

@rom1v Is it possible to implement a method or function that enables scrcpy to reach my phone using any TCP ports available?
It's annoying leaving my device to charge and then having my terminal say "connection refused" every time I launch scrcpy.
author: rdlf4

title: Video recording not working if killing process or closing window
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows 10
 - scrcpy version: 2.0, 2.1
 - installation method: windows release
 - device model: OnePlus 9
 - Android version: 11

**Describe the bug**
* Running `scrcpy -r test.mp4 --verbosity=debug` (using v2.1)

```
C:\Users\User\Downloads>scrcpy -r test.mp4 --verbosity=debug
scrcpy 2.1 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
DEBUG: ADB device found:
DEBUG:     -->   (usb)              215af537            device  LE2113
DEBUG: Device serial: 215af537
DEBUG: Using server (portable): C:\Program Files\scrcpy\scrcpy-server
C:\Program Files\scrcpy\scrcpy-server: 1 file pushed, 0 skipped. 78.2 MB/s (56955 bytes in 0.001s)
[server] INFO: Device: [OnePlus] OnePlus LE2113 (Android 11)
DEBUG: Server connected
DEBUG: Starting controller thread
INFO: Recording started to mp4 file: test.mp4
DEBUG: Starting receiver thread
[server] DEBUG: Using audio encoder: 'c2.android.opus.encoder'
[server] DEBUG: Using video encoder: 'c2.qti.avc.encoder'
INFO: Renderer: direct3d
DEBUG: Trilinear filtering disabled (not an OpenGL renderer
DEBUG: Using icon (portable): C:\Program Files\scrcpy\icon.png
DEBUG: Demuxer 'video': starting thread
DEBUG: Demuxer 'audio': starting thread
INFO: Texture: 1080x2400
WARN: [FFmpeg] track 1: codec frame size is not set
```

  * If pressing `Ctrl+C` (Windows), scrcpy terminates correctly and the recorded video works as expected
```
DEBUG: User requested to quit
DEBUG: quit...
DEBUG: Server disconnected
DEBUG: Server terminated
DEBUG: Demuxer 'audio': end of frames
DEBUG: Receiver stopped
INFO: Recording complete to mp4 file: test.mp4
DEBUG: Recorder thread ended
DEBUG: Demuxer 'video': end of frames
```

  * If killing the process (e.g., `taskkill /IM scrcpy.exe /F` or closing the `cmd` window), the video does not work properly. DIfferent behaviors
    * v2.0: the output video is 0 KB. Opening it with VLC yields an error: `Your input can't be opened`
    * v2.1: the output videos is >0 KB. Opening it with VLC yields no error, but the video has no duration (`--:--`) and does not play.

```
[server] DEBUG: Controller stopped
[server] DEBUG: Device message sender stopped
[server] DEBUG: Audio encoder stopped
[server] DEBUG: Screen streaming stopped
```

Having a graceful stop would be great!
I have no experience in C programming, but maybe there's a way of capturing the sigkill signal and treat it as a user input ctrl+c?
author: mattia-lecci

title: Bug: on v2.1 , when using "-S --always-on-top", closing the app's window will not automatically turn-on the display
body:
- [ V] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [ V] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: [e.g. Debian, Windows, macOS...]
 Windows 
 - scrcpy version: [e.g. 1.12.1]
scrcpy-win64-v2.1
 - installation method: [e.g. manual build, apt, snap, brew, Windows release...]
 ZIP
 - device model:
 Pixel 6
 - Android version: [e.g. 10]
13

**Describe the bug**
1. Connect to USB.
2. Run this :

C:\android\scrcpy\scrcpy-noconsole.vbs -S --always-on-top --stay-awake --no-audio

3. See that ScrCpy works fine, turning off the display of the device.
4. Close the window of ScrCpy . No need to disconnect from USB.

The bug is that The screen stays turned off.
A workaround is to turn it on, but then it gets turned on for a split second, and turns off, and I have to turn on again (and then it's fine).

Sadly I can't attach any logs, because the bug is after closing the window.
In the past, I had a different issue, that disconnecting from USB has let the screen stayed turned off, and sometimes I had to restart the device as it failed to turned on.

While bumping scrcpy to v2.1 for [Nixpkgs](https://github.com/nixos/nixpkgs) I encountered a behaviour change from v2.0 where the software fallback is gone from the renderer search logic. This may be an issue in SDL itself but I figured I'd start here.

title: Exception on main Thread
body:
[server] ERROR: Exception on thread Thread[main,5,main]
java.lang.IllegalStateException
	at android.media.MediaCodec.native_dequeueOutputBuffer(Native Method)
	at android.media.MediaCodec.dequeueOutputBuffer(MediaCodec.java:2698)
	at com.genymobile.scrcpy.ScreenEncoder.encode(ScreenEncoder.java:110)
	at com.genymobile.scrcpy.ScreenEncoder.internalStreamScreen(ScreenEncoder.java:91)
	at com.genymobile.scrcpy.ScreenEncoder.streamScreen(ScreenEncoder.java:65)
	at com.genymobile.scrcpy.Server.scrcpy(Server.java:93)
	at com.genymobile.scrcpy.Server.main(Server.java:309)
	at com.android.internal.os.RuntimeInit.nativeFinishInit(Native Method)
	at com.android.internal.os.RuntimeInit.main(RuntimeInit.java:340)
author: FaixyX

title: Crash on close if used --no-video-playback option
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows 7 x64 (yup, outdated)
 - scrcpy version: 2.1
 - installation method: Windows release
 - device model: Redmi Note 10 Pro
 - Android version: 11

**Describe the bug**

title: Bug: ScrCpy steals focus when orientation changes and it has no focus
body:
- [ V] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [V ] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

Original thread:
https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues/3947

**Environment**
 - OS: [e.g. Debian, Windows, macOS...]
 Windows
 - scrcpy version: [e.g. 1.12.1]
 2.1
 - installation method: [e.g. manual build, apt, snap, brew, Windows release...]
 ZIP
 - device model:
 Pixel 6
 - Android version: [e.g. 10]
 13

**Describe the bug**
1. Open ScrCpy using these flags:

```
C:\android\scrcpy\scrcpy-noconsole.vbs -S --always-on-top --stay-awake --no-audio
```

2. Run some app that will change the orientation every now and then, or use adb to do it somehow in the background using a script that does the same. 

Here's a sample app to simulate this, which changes the orientation after 5 seconds since shown. 

[My Application.zip](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/files/11314795/My.Application.zip)
[app-debug.zip](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/files/11314796/app-debug.zip)

Code:


```
class MainActivity : AppCompatActivity() {
    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
        setContentView(R.layout.activity_main)
        Handler(mainLooper).postDelayed({
            requestedOrientation = if (resources.configuration.orientation == ActivityInfo.SCREEN_ORIENTATION_PORTRAIT)
                ActivityInfo.SCREEN_ORIENTATION_LANDSCAPE
            else
                ActivityInfo.SCREEN_ORIENTATION_PORTRAIT
        },  5000L)
    }
}
```

3. Open some app on Windows that needs focus, such as a game in full screen or a video player that's in full screen or on top.
4. Wait for the Android app (or script) to change the orientation while you use the app on Windows that should stay in focus.

The bug is that ScrCpy steals the focus because of it, as you can see here:

https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/assets/543275/f03ee5e3-6b14-4508-b953-bbcb317eb22e

[2023-04-25_00-42-13_1.zip](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/files/11314895/2023-04-25_00-42-13_1.zip)

body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Debian 12
 - scrcpy version: 2.1
 - installation method: manual build from release 2.1
 - device model: irrelevant
 - Android version: irrelevant

**Describe the bug**
Installation prodecure with install_release.sh (release 2.1) downloads scrcpy-server 2.0. Resulting installation will not work due to mismatch between scrcpy (2.1) and scrcpy-server (2.0)



```
grep ^PREBUILT_SERVER_URL scrcpy-2.1/install_release.sh
PREBUILT_SERVER_URL=https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/releases/download/v2.0/scrcpy-server-v2.0
```

```
scrcpy 2.1 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
scrcpy/scrcpy-server: 1 file pushed, 0 skipped. 7.6 MB/s (52867 bytes in 0.007s)
[server] ERROR: Exception on thread Thread[main,5,main]
java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: The server version (2.0) does not match the client (2.1)
        at com.genymobile.scrcpy.Server.createOptions(Server.java:172)
        at com.genymobile.scrcpy.Server.main(Server.java:360)
        at com.android.internal.os.RuntimeInit.nativeFinishInit(Native Method)
        at com.android.internal.os.RuntimeInit.main(RuntimeInit.java:355)
ERROR: Server connection failed
```

One workaround is to manually download scrcpy-server 2.1 from the release page but perhaps this could be fixed.

title: Feature Request: Simultaneous Control of Multiple Android Devices for Synchronized Device Management
body:
- [X] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Currently, there is no straightforward way to control multiple Android devices simultaneously using a single controlling device. This can be challenging for scenarios where synchronized interactions are required across multiple devices.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would like scrcpy to have a feature that allows for the replication of taps and swipes from a single controlling device to multiple connected Android devices. This feature would enable synchronized interactions across multiple devices, providing a convenient solution for testing, demonstrations, and presentations.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
One alternative solution that offers multi-device control is [Total Control by Sigma-rt](https://www.sigma-rt.com/en/tc/). Total Control is a software tool designed specifically for controlling and managing multiple Android devices simultaneously. It provides features such as screen mirroring, remote control, and synchronized operations across multiple devices.

Other similar tools and services that offer multi-device control include:

- **Genymotion Cloud**: Genymotion Cloud is a cloud-based Android emulator platform that allows you to create and manage multiple virtual Android devices. It offers collaboration features, including synchronized testing and device management.

- **TestProject**: [TestProject](https://www.testim.io/testim-mobile/) is a test automation platform that provides multi-device testing capabilities. It allows you to control and interact with multiple physical or virtual devices simultaneously for testing purposes.


**Additional context**
This feature would be highly beneficial for various use cases, such as app testing where multiple devices need to be controlled simultaneously to ensure consistent behavior across different devices. It would also be valuable for educational purposes, allowing instructors to showcase app functionality simultaneously on multiple devices during presentations or demonstrations.

> All written content was generated by OpenAI ChatGPT, But the Idea must be clear with it's intention. Incase you have any questions or need extra clarification, please feel free to ask. I would be happy to support.

anyway its not a big problem just kind of annoying:)

author: mrpbpp

title: scrcpy enables web support
body:
There is a well-known project [https://ya-webadb.vercel.app](https://ya-webadb.vercel.app) that can support scrcpy web, but this project needs to connect to adb through usb or websockify.
Of course, the use of usb needs to be limited by the data cable, and when using websockify, it also needs to be signed by a trusted certificate to be able to access it on other devices.
If scrcpy natively enables web support, perhaps these problems can be solved.At the moment when ipv6 covers a large area, it may be possible to realize real remote control.

author: plia7

title: Programmatically Send Keypress Through OTG
body:
- [X] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

Good day! Firstly, I would like to apologize for this lengthy feature request.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Hello! I got myself a "new" phone which is a hand-me-down from my mom. I have been experimenting with it for about two days on how to debloat the whole thing with as little friction as possible, such as that I don't need to root the device and the likes. And so far, it works! Except, my script actually removes some vital components in the android system, and I need to be able to reformat the phone so that it reverts back to its initial state, and to remove the thingy that removes the important thingy. But doing that is tedious! First, I have to reformat the phone, then have to go through the init of the phone. Which basically means signing in on google, getting thru hoops and loops of EULA and the shitty "install these apps".

Now, I have been using this program to be able to control my phone without using my sweaty hands to do so. And for the past 3 years, it had worked flawlessly! And lately, I just discovered OTG mode, and it is actually super useful for devices that does not have USB debugging enabled, and which screens I can't control due to it being broken. It had actually saved me multiple times now!

And now, we're back to the present day, in this predicament at hand. I have combed the internet for answers on "how to use my linux machine as a keyboard for a device" or some variant of the question ("linux emulate usb slave" and the likes). Which, as a dumb person, thought would be actually out there, as I have been clearly not the first person to thought of this. But as you would have guessed, I'm just a lowly python programmer that so happens to really like use arch linux as a form of sadomasochism.

Yes, I've found a solution, but I do not know how to implement just that. It's like an apple pie recipe, but instead, I got pointed to a lengthy autobiography of a wheat farmer that in 176 pages tells you how the wheat is turned to flour. But that's not what I want!

And thus, we have come to this. Can I request to have a feature where I can programmatically send keypress through the OTG?

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Basically, I want to be able to have an API where I can either send key codes or an ASCII character to, and have it sent to the device thru OTG. Other proposal would be to have a CLI command that takes in a key code or ASCII character, then do the same thing.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I have thought of using a raspberry pi zero or raspberry pi pico, but I have steered away from it due to the complexity it would add to my already complicated workflow. But now, it seems that either I have to do it that way, or have this feature request added in. Nay, I have one more option: to learn C. But this was supposed to be a quick and dirty 7 day project. But alas, as my future self would find out, it would not be!

Run scrcpy initially with the console and disappear as soon as the pecking window opens, with some command line that allows the user to choose if, when an error occurs, the console closes or remains open.

Wait some time after trying to run scrcpy without console and if the mirror does not open, I thought that possibly there was an error or open normally with the console, because I hope to know if there was an error or not, but the console will be there.

When creating a launch script with those already defined in a ".cmd" or ".bat" file (on windows) but expect only a mirroring window, without the console open/minimized while mirroring.

title: Audio cracking, robotic sound on MacOS with Meta Quest Pro (Related to Unity Best Latency setting)
body:
- [ ] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [ ] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

There have been reports on cracking audio, some of them have been (partly) resolved:

https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues/3793
https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues/4055

This issue ticket here is also for audio crackling but for this specific configuration:

**Environment**
 - OS: MacOS 12.6.6, 
 - scrcpy version: 2.1
 - installation method: brew
 - device model: MacBook 14" M1 Max
 - Android version: Meta Quest Pro, Android 12, Meta Quest-Build 54.0

**Describe the bug**
I am mirroring my audio app [OpenSoundLab](https://github.com/ludzeller/opensoundlab). The image seems fine as usual, but audio is never clean and glitch free. Sounds like plenty of buffer underruns. I have increased both audio buffers up to seconds, but it doesnt change the glitches. Very low settings make it worse though. author: ludzeller

title: Scrcpy not starting
body:
- [X] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing[issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Fedora 38
 - scrcpy version: 2.1
 - installation method: copr repo
 - device model: redmi note 8, xiaomi mi 9, samsung s9+
 - Android version: 11, 11, 10

**Describe the bug**
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

On errors, please provide the output of the console (and `adb logcat` if relevant).

```
[n1lby73@localhost ~]$ scrcpy

[server] INFO: Device: [Xiaomi] xiaomi Redmi Note 8 (Android 11)

Please do not post screenshots of your terminal, just post the content as text instead.

title: Failed to start audio capture / Demuxer 'audio': stream explicitly disabled by the device on Android 11
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

 - OS: Ubuntu
 - scrcpy version: 2.1
 - installation method: manual build
 - device model: ASUS
 - Android version: 11 (LOS-based Custom ROM)

Sound does not work by default. As I've read in several other issues regarding sound, you bring up the Shell Heap Dump report activity for a short period of time to make android (11) think that scrcpy - which has the same uid - would be running upfront. That does not work on my device (ofc. with the screen on).
If I, however, run `adb shell "am start com.android.shell/.HeapDumpActivity"` before I run scrcpy, it does work. (The Heap Dump report activity pops up, scrcpy starts, closes it and audio works as expected.) Below I will post the output of what scrcpy logs without the manually started activity:
```
scrcpy 2.1 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
scrcpy-server-v2.1: 1 file pushed. 1.4 MB/s (56955 bytes in 0.040s)
[server] INFO: Device: [asus] asus ASUS_[redacted] (Android 11)
INFO: Renderer: opengl
INFO: OpenGL version: 4.6 (Compatibility Profile) Mesa 23.1.3
INFO: Trilinear filtering enabled
INFO: Texture: 1080x2336
[server] ERROR: Failed to start audio capture
[server] ERROR: On Android 11, audio capture must be started in the foreground, make sure that the device is unlocked when starting scrcpy.
WARN: Demuxer 'audio': stream explicitly disabled by the device
```

When searching for similar Issues, I found #3796 which looked a lot like my issue. I therefore tried to apply the same fix which was successfull there: Setting `SystemClock.sleep()` to a higher value (I tried `500` ms). After that I recompiled the scrcpy-server according to https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/doc/build.md#option-1-build-everything-from-sources (which is a real PITA btw, thank you for delivering that one pre-build by default.) and didnt notice any difference. On the other hand I have no experience with java whatsoever so I might have done some mistakes when recompiling.

title: Support displaying background tasks in scrcpy
body:
- [ ] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

Description:
Currently, scrcpy is an excellent tool for mirroring the screen of an Android device to a computer. However, it only displays the active foreground screen, and there is no way to view or interact with background tasks running on the device.

Feature Request:
I would like to request the implementation of a feature that allows scrcpy to display background tasks as well. This would be particularly useful for developers and testers who need to monitor the behavior of their apps when they are running in the background.

Possible Implementation:
One possible implementation could be to introduce an option or a command that enables the display of background tasks in scrcpy. When this option is enabled, scrcpy would continuously capture and mirror the screen of the device, including any background tasks that are currently running. This would provide a more comprehensive view of the device's activity and help in identifying any issues or bugs related to background tasks.

Benefits:
1. Developers and testers can monitor the behavior and performance of their apps when they are running in the background.
2. It would enable better debugging and identification of issues related to background tasks.
3. Provides a more comprehensive view of the device's activity, improving overall testing and troubleshooting capabilities.

Additional Notes:
It's important to consider any potential performance implications or limitations of capturing and displaying background tasks. Implementing this feature may require collaboration with the Android OS or utilizing specific APIs to access background tasks.

Thank you for considering this feature request. I believe it would greatly enhance the functionality of scrcpy and make it an even more valuable tool for Android developers and testers.

title: Scrcpy with two usb devices on remote host (more than one device/emulator)
body:
I know that this problem is pretty old and there was lot of issues with it that was solved. But mine looks like new one as my enviropment is slighter different.
I have two PCs in local network. PC1 (with local ip 192.168.50.45) has two phones connected via usb and I want to connect with scrcpy from PC2 to both of this phones.
So I started `adb -a nodaemon server` on PC1 and can see device list from PC2. 
It looks like this (both phones has same model):
```
(usb)               p000000            device  Mi_A2
(usb)               p000001            device  Mi_A2
```

But when I try to connect with this command `scrcpy --tunnel-host=192.168.50.45 --serial=p000000` I get lot of same errors in a row (with different ports):
```
adb.exe: error: more than one device/emulator
ERROR: "adb forward" returned with value 1
WARN: Could not forward port 27183, retrying on 27184
```

If I disconnect one phone from PC1 (only this change) - I can connect to another phone with same command as above without problem.
As you can see - in my case it doesn't help to write `--serial=p000000` as I still getting errors with two connected phones.

Any ideas what could be wrong?

title: windows: otg + tcpip = hid support
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist. edit: not hard enough.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
On windows, the hid keyboard and mouse only works on otg mode.
Because you can only have one device at a time connected via usb on windows. (As said in the docs)

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I think, if you combine the tcpip feature, so the usb is not used.
And otg, so the keyboard and mouse connect.
it could have hid support, with lag, to windows!

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
You can recreate a similar effect, by:
1. open a console
2. run `scrcpy --otg` (you may need to use the `-s` flag)
3. when it runs, tab off
4. create another console window
5. In that window, connect to the phone wirelessly. (with the `wireless debugging` option in settings)
6. have the otg window next to the other window
7. click on the otg window
8. Have fun!

https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/assets/64162703/c86af807-1159-480c-93c3-ee215cf7bb9d

**Additional context**

author: chieonol

title: Use phone as a microphone
body:
- [x] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

I use my phone as a mic for my desktop by setting `--audio-source=mic` and then using Pipewire to connect it to a virtual source. It would be a lot easier to have some flag that does this automatically since it also plays whatever the mic picks up. Using `--no-audio-playback` doesn't work because I also disable video, so I also have to use Pipewire to disable the mic audio from being played.

body:

 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Raspberry Pi OS Lite (64-bit)
   - Release date: May 3rd 2023
   - System: 64-bit
   - Kernel version: 6.1
   - Debian version: 11 (bullseye)
 - scrcpy version: 2.1.1 (from GitHub)
   - Dependencies (compiled / linked):
   - SDL: 2.0.14 / 2.0.14
   - libavcodec: 58.91.100 / 58.91.100
   - libavformat: 58.45.100 / 58.45.100
   - libavutil: 56.51.100 / 56.51.100
   - libavdevice: 58.10.100 / 58.10.100
   - libusb: - / 1.0.24
 - installation method: Manual via "Latest version" (https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/doc/linux.md)
 - device model: Google Pixel 6a
 - Android version: 13

**Describe the bug**
If the Raspberry Pi is configured to output audio on HDMI0, the following error occurs:
```
$ scrcpy
scrcpy 2.1.1 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
INFO: ADB device found:
INFO:     --> (tcpip)  172.18.2.44:5555                device  Pixel_6a
/usr/local/share/scrcpy/scrcpy-server: 1 file pushed. 2.0 MB/s (56995 bytes in 0.027s)
[server] INFO: Device: [Google] google Pixel 6a (Android 13)
INFO: Renderer: opengl
INFO: OpenGL version: 2.1 Mesa 20.3.5
WARN: Trilinear filtering disabled (OpenGL 3.0+ or ES 2.0+ required
INFO: Texture: 1080x2400
ERROR: Could not open audio device: ALSA: Couldn't find any hardware audio formats
ERROR: Demuxer error
```
Before I switched to HDMI0 the video was displayed without audio (because set to audio jack).

`scrcpy --no-audio` works with HDMI0:
```
$ scrcpy --no-audio
scrcpy 2.1.1 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
INFO: ADB device found:
INFO:     --> (tcpip)  172.18.2.44:5555                device  Pixel_6a
/usr/local/share/scrcpy/scrcpy-server: 1 file pushed. 3.2 MB/s (56995 bytes in 0.017s)
[server] INFO: Device: [Google] google Pixel 6a (Android 13)
INFO: Renderer: opengl
INFO: OpenGL version: 2.1 Mesa 20.3.5
WARN: Trilinear filtering disabled (OpenGL 3.0+ or ES 2.0+ required
INFO: Texture: 1080x2400
```
Do you have any ideas?

Thanks in advance!

- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md). - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues). **Environment** - OS: Ubuntu 22.04.2 LTS - scrcpy version: 2.1.1 - installation method: $ sudo apt install scrcpy - device model: Nokia 100X TA-1399 - Android version: Android 11 04US_1_650 **Describe the bug** ``` Returning "ERROR: Exception on thread Thread[main,5,main] java.lang.IllegalArgumentException. ``` I'm not sure of what this exactly means, but I will paste the terminal output.

I type scrcpy, and I receive this:

``` scrcpy 1.21 * daemon not running; starting now at tcp:5037 * daemon started successfully /usr/share/scrcpy/scrcpy-server: 1 fil...shed. 2.6 MB/s (39473 bytes in 0.014s) [server] INFO: Device: HMD Global Nokia X100 (Android 11) INFO: Renderer: opengl INFO: OpenGL version: 4.6 (Compatibility Profile) Mesa 23.0.4-0ubuntu1~22.04.1 INFO: Trilinear filtering enabled INFO: Initial texture: 1080x2400 WARN: Device disconnected [server] ERROR: Exception on thread Thread[main,5,main] java.lang.IllegalArgumentException at android.media.MediaCodec.native_configure(Native Method) at android.media.MediaCodec.configure(MediaCodec.java:2127) at android.media.MediaCodec.configure(MediaCodec.java:2043) at com.genymobile.scrcpy.ScreenEncoder.configure(ScreenEncoder.java:233) at com.genymobile.scrcpy.ScreenEncoder.internalStreamScreen(ScreenEncoder.java:86) at com.genymobile.scrcpy.ScreenEncoder.streamScreen(ScreenEncoder.java:65) at com.genymobile.scrcpy.Server.scrcpy(Server.java:93) at com.genymobile.scrcpy.Server.main(Server.java:309) at com.android.internal.os.RuntimeInit.nativeFinishInit(Native Method) at com.android.internal.os.RuntimeInit.main(RuntimeInit.java:399) WARN: Killing the server... ``` I have gone ahead and done adb kill-server and tried again. I have verified/reinstalled the program. I just updated my phone, nothing is working fully. If anyone knows anything about this, please let me know. I'm probably doing something wrong, but who knows... I have tried everything!

title: How can I get / calculate the exact timestamp (device) of the first frame recorded?
body:
- [X ] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
After capturing videos using `scrcpy`, we want to be able to get the EXACT **timestamp** of the first frame captured on **the device**. 
We've been checking different mechanisms to get that date, but without success. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Probably it will be nice to log the timestamp of the first frame once when `firstFrameSent = true`?

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
If anybody has any WA for this I will be happy to try it, also to send a PR if this is useful to somebody else. 


Thanks!! 🙏

if scrcpy server can broadcast the touch inputs from controller, then we can build third party android apps, which will listen to those broadcast events and display a cursor using a floating window on android

title: Sound is in slow motion and only coming from tablet speakers
body:
- [x] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [x] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Windows (PC)
 - scrcpy version: 2.1.1
 - installation method: 
 - device model: Razer Edge Wifi
 - Android version: 12

When I connect my Razer Edge, the video feed comes through on the PC perfectly with no lag. The audio does not come through the PC at all and continues to come through the Edge, and is significantly slowed down. It sounds like it is in slow motion. The cooling fans on the edge also ramp up rapidly. I tried searching and did not see anyone else having this exact issue. I tried all of the audio codecs, and the problem remains. Thanks for any help!

author: Grafcube

title: ERROR: Server connection failed
body:
> scrcpy 2.1.1 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
INFO: ADB device found:
INFO:     -->   (usb)      520005654e3995fd            device  SM_J600F
C:\platform-tools\scrcpy-server: 1 file pushed, 0 skipped. 12.8 MB/s (56995 bytes in 0.004s)
ERROR: Server connection failed
Press any key to continue . . .

Getting the above error when launching scrcpy-console.bat. Launching scrcpy.exe opens terminal and instantly closes it.
Trying this on galaxy j6 running Arrow Os android 12.
author: at644
